maps.one_liner: Early-stage initialization, UI text rendering, sprite animation, and scene logic dispatch — mostly class ctors/dtors and the main game loop.

subsystems.name: Game Loop/Scene Logic Dispatch

representative_vas.role: Main game loop state machine; switches on scene ID and routes interaction (5077 bytes)
Scene action handler with damage, treasure, animation triggering (1640 bytes)
Multi-NPC interaction dispatcher, state syncing (1032 bytes)

subsystems.notes: Heavy use of in_ECX (this) pointer and DAT_008a9b50 global state manager. Scene switch at offset +0x1d4 selects behavior (0x11300, 0x1124c, 0x11332 etc are internal scene IDs). All return error codes (6 = exit). Vtable method calls dominate (FUN_00557370 damage, FUN_0055cfc0 effects, FUN_00556380 status). Port difficulty: HIGH — many cross-subsystem dependencies, serialized game state.

subsystems.name: Sprite Animation State Machine

representative_vas.role: Set character sprite state + animation param (232 bytes)
Reset character sprite to idle/standing state (222 bytes)
Compose sprite with dialog/cutscene frame parameters (431 bytes)

subsystems.notes: Manages 32x sprite slots (0x14 bytes each) at offsets +0x2fc, +0x304, +0x308. Sprite render state at +0x15b64 (1=talk,2=damage,3=stand), +0x15b70 (x-offset), +0x15b74 (y-offset). Particle effect fields at +0x15b8c, +0x15b90. Port difficulty: MEDIUM — straightforward state mutation, but requires correct sprite object model.

subsystems.name: UI Text Rendering / Font System

representative_vas.role: Gigantic font/encoding lookup, glyph width calc, multi-byte support (7330 bytes)
Text string encoder, character width accumulation (815 bytes)
Message/item description formatter with sprintf-like behavior (3244 bytes)

subsystems.notes: Global font tables at DAT_005cd978 (+0xa4 per glyph, null-terminated). String arrays with 0x24-byte slots. Heavy use of character encoding (bytes <0x80 are ASCII, >=0x80 are two-byte). Port difficulty: HIGH — requires full font metric recovery and multi-byte character set handling.

subsystems.name: Object Pool & Initialization

representative_vas.role: __thiscall ctor; allocates and zero-initializes all game object pools (1175 bytes)
Single-object initializer, zeroes particle/effect fields (54 bytes)

subsystems.notes: 0x4017d0 is THE main ctor: allocates 64x 600-byte sprites, 32x 0x15bb0-byte map objects, 128x 0x2fc entities, 1024x 0x284 items, 96x 0x20c particles, 128x 0x1f8 projectiles. Also allocates large buffers: 0x2cb044 (particle pool), 0xa52c, 0xb2c (scratch), plus 5x 0xc-byte string buffers at [0x9e2..0x9e6]. Uses global DAT_008a9b50 singleton. Port difficulty: CRITICAL — defines memory layout of entire object system.

subsystems.name: Helper: Sprite Depth Sort & Position

representative_vas.role: Calculate z-order (depth) between two character sprites by y-pos (100 bytes)
Fetch current active sprite count (47 bytes)

subsystems.notes: Simple Z-order calc at sprite.y + sprite.height/2. Used for render ordering. Port difficulty: LOW — standalone utility.

subsystems.name: UI/Dialog Composition

representative_vas.role: Sprite + dialog params dispatcher (431 bytes)
Screen fade/transition effect (503 bytes)

subsystems.notes: Routes sprite + dialog box composition via global state at DAT_008a9b50 + 0x2790 (NPC list). Port difficulty: MEDIUM — mostly dispatch logic.

quirks.title: Object Pool Layout Hardcoded in Ctor
quirks.desc: Single 1175-byte ctor allocates and initializes the entire game object system via nested loops over fixed counts (64, 32, 128, 1024, 96, 128). Sprite offsets, entity sizes, and item counts are burn-in constants; changing pool sizes requires recompiling the ctor. DAT_008a9b50 is the master singleton holding all pointers.

quirks.title: Sprite State Encoding Collision
quirks.desc: Sprite state (talk=1, damage=2, stand=3) is stored separately from animation frame (at +0x15b70/74/7c/80/84/88). State is checked before running animation logic, but there's no multi-frame animation — only slot-based lookup. Quirky design makes frame reuse difficult.

quirks.title: Game Logic Dispatch via Scene ID Hash
quirks.desc: Main loop switches on *(int*)(in_ECX + 0x1d4), which holds scene IDs like 0x11300, 0x1124c, 0x11256, 0x1126b, 0x114a4 — these are not sequential or named in-code, only switch cases. Reverse-eng must use brute-force hash lookup or trace all callers.

quirks.title: Font Glyph Table Global at DAT_005cd978
quirks.desc: Each glyph entry is 0xa4 bytes (164 decimal), null-term separated. The entire font table is a single hardcoded global; no dynamic font loading. Font metrics (width, height, baseline) are burned into the table structure, making custom fonts or scaling impossible without full table rebuild.

quirks.title: Recursive Sprite Iteration with 0x20 Slot Hardcoding
quirks.desc: Sprites are stored in slots 0..0x1f (32 total), with linear search on every interaction. No hash table or sparse representation; this is O(32) per sprite lookup. Port must preserve this limit or rewrite slot management.

maps.one_liner: Menu/UI controller, dialog boxes, inventory management, and sprite rendering functions for the game interface.

subsystems.name: Menu/Dialog Controller

representative_vas.role: Menu stack push/frame allocation
Character stats display formatter
Dialog box creation and item list display
Item storage/item selection manager

subsystems.notes: Menu system uses frame stack at in_ECX+0x4e/0x4c, references vtable at in_ECX+0x48. Heavy use of DAT_008a9b6c (UI string buffer), DAT_00854990 (strings), DAT_008a9534. Frames manage item visibility via flags (byte at offset 0x201a), and maintain list selection via offset +0x174. Port requires mapping string indices and UI constant offsets.

subsystems.name: Character State Initialization

representative_vas.role: Character creation/stats setup (27KB monster)
Character stat progression and ability updates
Save/load file path construction

subsystems.notes: FUN_0041f200 is a large initialization function using in_ECX as character object pointer, with heavy use of array-indexed offsets (0x5xxx, 0x6xxx ranges) for stat slots. Tracks character level, ability flags, item arrays. References ability data at DAT_006b6ea8 (stride 0xd, entries 0x34 bytes). Port difficulty: HIGH — depends on exact struct layout and magic offset constants.

subsystems.name: Sprite/Asset Rendering Preparation

representative_vas.role: Sprite palette/effect color adjustment per battle difficulty
Sprite slot initialization (thiscall, 194 bytes)
Menu grid/icon list rendering prep (2529 bytes)

subsystems.notes: FUN_00417c40 reads effect flags from in_ECX+0x289c (difficulty mode), applies color filters (opacity %, hue shifts). Uses local 1024-byte buffers for sprite palette tables. FUN_004178e0 is a thiscall on ar_sprite_slot struct. References offset +0x18e0 (sprite descriptor array), +0xaac (slot pointer array). Port: medium — struct offsets critical, no vtables visible, mostly procedural image manipulation.

subsystems.name: Shop/NPC Dialog Logic

representative_vas.role: Healer/Inn visitor interaction handler (1919 bytes)
Menu window/list box rendering (770 bytes)
General NPC dialog framework

subsystems.notes: FUN_0041c390 demonstrates dialog state machine: branches on param_3 (1=Healer, 2+=Inn), allocates strings locally (512-byte buffers), calls FUN_0055f190 for input, switches on response codes (0xb, 0xc, 0xd). Uses in_ECX+0x4064 for location ID (checks value 10 for special currency handling). Port: medium — needs string table mapping, input event codes.

subsystems.name: Item Grid/List Management

representative_vas.role: Item deposit dialog (920 bytes)
Item entry UI initialization in list grid
Item list text population from template strings (3103 bytes)

subsystems.notes: Item lists use 0x10-byte entries in pool at in_ECX+0x17c; grid has capacity at +0x174+0x4. FUN_00410610 copies string templates (DAT_00854994, DAT_00854990, DAT_00854988) at 100-byte strides. Uses in_ECX+0x16c as grid cursor, +0x168 as capacity marker. References character data at in_ECX+0xbbc for equipped item IDs.

subsystems.name: Save File Management

representative_vas.role: Save/load filename construction with type dispatch (3030 bytes)
Save timestamp/metadata handler

subsystems.notes: FUN_0041a890 switches on param_1 (save slot IDs 1, 0x271a, 0x2724, 0x272e, 0x2725) to construct paths like 'savedata_02d.bak/dat/sdt'. Uses large local buffers (512-byte strings) for path construction. References GetSystemTime. Port: low — mostly string/path manipulation, no complex state.

quirks.title: Magic offset array stride in character data
quirks.desc: Character ability lookup uses stride 0xd (13 bytes) with secondary table at 0x34-byte intervals: (&DAT_006b6ea8)[(uVar13 & 0xffff) * 0xd] versus &UNK_006b6eac + (uVar13 & 0xffff) * 0x34. This suggests two interleaved data tables (one for IDs, one for properties) — not uncommon in engines but requires careful struct recovery.

quirks.title: Hardcoded character stat slot widths (0x5xxx range)
quirks.desc: Function zeros 118 consecutive int32 fields starting in_ECX+0x5634, then initializes ability/item arrays at specific 4-byte strides. The sheer number of magic offsets (0x56d8, 0x5643, 0x5683, etc.) suggests a monolithic character struct at ~24KB. Each offset likely maps to a gameplay stat (HP, MP, skills, inventory, party data).

quirks.title: String copy using length-prefix loop unroll
quirks.desc: Strings are copied via a loop that counts down a negated byte-length, then unrolls as 4-byte then 1-byte copies. This is a compact memcpy pattern but adds instruction noise — likely MSVC CRT memcpy inlining. Every string copy uses this pattern; recognizing it aids port.

quirks.title: Difficulty-dependent stat formula branches
quirks.desc: Character initialization checks a global switch at DAT_008a9b50+0x2780 (difficulty mode: 10, 0x14, 0x28, 0x32) and applies different stat formulas for damage scaling. No obfuscation, but the +0x2780 offset into a global struct requires careful reverse-engineering to locate the difficulty field.

quirks.title: Unsigned-masked array indexing throughout
quirks.desc: All array accesses use (index & 0xffff) even though loops clearly fit in 16 bits. This is a decompiler artifact (masking to match the original ushort type) but indicates the binary extensively uses uint16 for counters — important for cycle-accurate port.

quirks.title: Thiscall member functions embedded in large structs
quirks.desc: FUN_004178e0 and FUN_004178b0 are both __thiscall methods on ar_sprite_slot (struct with vtable at +0x48 implied). These are method calls, not global functions, but live in a flat namespace. Port needs to reconstruct class methods from scattered entry points.

maps.one_liner: Scene/level initialization and entity spawning code; large data-driven setup functions writing context object fields with palette/graphics/actor data.

subsystems.name: Scene Initialization

representative_vas.role: Boss/final area scene init; palette setup, camera defaults, entity pool init
Generic dungeon/area scene init; entity list and renderer setup
Big switch dispatcher for entity/ability spawning by ID (0x4e86-0x4ea8 range)
Alternative area scene init variant
Event/narrative scene init with specific entity setup
Scene init with conditional palette branches (0xc747/0xc745/0x1875a checks)

subsystems.notes: Class/vtable: None visible (pure procedural); Key globals: DAT_008a9b6c (scene name string), DAT_006826c0+ (entity definition table, stride 0xc0), DAT_0062a400/DAT_0062a598 (shared sprite/palette bases). Key offsets: in_ECX+0x15914/0x15918/0x1591c (palette pointers), in_ECX+0xc754 (entity count, ushort), in_ECX+0xca28 (actor array base, stride 0x140). Port difficulty: Medium - struct initialization is straightforward but requires mapping 50+ offset constants; entity spawning has complex conditionals.

subsystems.name: NPC/Character Setup

representative_vas.role: Sprite frame & anchor initialization; sets position/dimensions/rendering mode
NPC template lookup and name string copy from global table
Battle difficulty mode switcher with RNG-based parameter randomization

subsystems.notes: Class/vtable: None; Uses procedural setup. Key globals: DAT_008a9b6c (NPC data base), DAT_00682660 (difficulty tables, stride 6). Key offsets: in_ECX+0x48/0x4a (frame index), in_ECX+0x50 (asset pointer), in_ECX+0xc834/0xc838 (battle position), in_ECX+0xc874 (difficulty param). Quirk: Uses FUN_005bf505 (RNG) per difficulty, manual memcpy for name strings. Port difficulty: Low-medium.

subsystems.name: Entity/Actor Definition Lookup

representative_vas.role: Look up enemy/actor definition by ID; returns pointer to 0xc0-stride record
Alternative entity lookup; loads parameters from global tables (DAT_006826c0 array)
Tiny (68-byte) inline entity ID lookup helper

subsystems.notes: Class/vtable: None; Pure lookups into global arrays. Key globals: DAT_006826c0 (entity ID array, stride 0xc0 per entry, contains nested 0x180-stride sub-arrays for parameters). Lookups iterate until ID 0. Port difficulty: Low - just array walking.

subsystems.name: Entity Pool & Context Management

representative_vas.role: Set per-scene parameters (72-byte inline setter used by all scene inits)
Large (1115 bytes) context builder for complex scene type
Scene context init variant (1119 bytes)

subsystems.notes: Class/vtable: None; Global context is single in_ECX object. Key offsets: in_ECX+0xc754 (entity count, incremented per spawn), in_ECX+[0..0x14]*0x14+0x48 (per-entity name override), in_ECX+0xca28+index*0x140 (actor array). Entity stride is 0x300 bytes. Port difficulty: Low - straightforward offset-based parameter setting.

quirks.title: Hand-Rolled strlen + Aligned memcpy for Scene Names
quirks.desc: All major scene inits manually copy scene name string from DAT_008a9b6c to entity records at offsets +0x758 and +0x7a8 using a loop that counts down from 0xffffffff until null, then copies 4 bytes at a time. This is inefficient but ensures 4-byte alignment.

quirks.title: Entity Record Stride is 0x300 Bytes
quirks.desc: Entities indexed as `uVar2 * 0x300 + in_ECX` suggest fixed 0x300-byte records. Actor state is stored at different stride (0x140) in separate 0xca28 array, suggesting entities and actors are separate pools.

quirks.title: Magic Sentinel 0xf00000 for Uninitialized State
quirks.desc: Entity init routines set offset +0x814 = 0xf00000; used as marker for 'default/uninitialized'. Conditionals later check (value == 0xf00000) and optionally override.

quirks.title: Negative Fixed-Point Coordinates in Camera Setup
quirks.desc: Camera/world bounds initialized with values like 0xfffffce0 (=-544), 0xfffff9c0 (=-1600), 0xffff63c0 (=-32320 in 0x42da50). These are signed negative offsets in two's complement, likely camera viewport bounds or world origin offsets.

quirks.title: Global Palette Pointer Tables per Scene
quirks.desc: Each scene init writes different DAT_* palette pointers to in_ECX+0x15914/0x15918/0x1591c. These are scene-specific tilemap/palette data. The same offset is reused but filled with different global addresses.

quirks.title: RNG-Based Difficulty Parameter Scaling
quirks.desc: Difficulty mode 2+ branches call FUN_005bf505 (likely Mersenne Twister) in a loop, multiplying random output by constants (0x32, 100) and adding base offsets (0x3b6, 0x28a, etc.) to scale challenge values. No difficulty mode 1+ without RNG.

maps.one_liner: Frame logic, battle initialization, game state management, and menu/input handling clustered around large scenario setup and turn execution functions.

subsystems.name: Battle Scenario Initializer

representative_vas.role: Main scenario init dispatcher (25293B) — routes 0x111d4-0x111e8 mode IDs to party/map/difficulty setup
Subscenario helper (599B) — sets up character/NPC spawn tables
Map dimension configurator (266B) — establishes play area boundaries

subsystems.notes: Heavy use of indexed field initialization via in_ECX pointer (this-call). Stages 0x294-byte party slots with 8 character capacity (stride 0x11 = count field). Map limits at offsets 0x6c-0x7f, difficulty tables at 0xb8-0xba (DAT_00693808). Port hazard: raw offset writes demand byte-level verification.

subsystems.name: Battle Turn Engine

representative_vas.role: Turn state machine (8866B) — processes character animation frames, status effect ticks, damage/heal resolution
Damage/action resolver (2120B) — evaluates skill hit, calculates HP/MP deltas, manages revive states
Character action queue (302B) — queues next battle action from input
Pathfinding validator (via call sites) — checks walkability on 3-tier difficulty grids

subsystems.notes: Uses switch-driven state machine: states 0-5+ correspond to combat phases. Timer management at offsets +0x1c/+0x20 (GetTickCount-based 100-300ms debounce). Grid collision indexed by (uVar6 & 0xffff) into tilemap at 0x2c1030. Offset 0x5653 stores difficulty (1=easy, 3=hard). Load-bearing: modulo arithmetic on slot count (offset 0x3) may have off-by-ones; needs verification.

subsystems.name: Battle Grid & Collision

representative_vas.role: Collision/grid setup (5491B) — validates spawn tiles, pathfinding on 0x80-stride grid
Tile query helper (228B) — evaluates walkability at grid coordinates
Pathfind wrapper (1091B) — breadth-first on obstacle grid

subsystems.notes: Grid stride 0x80 per row. Offsets +0x4/+0xc/+0x8 = x/y/width. Map storage at DAT_008a9b50+0x1048 (indirect through globals). Port signal: collision checks compare against 1 (walkable); off-by-one risk in boundary clamping.

subsystems.name: Game State & Mode Init

representative_vas.role: Global game state setup (5039B) — initializes sound levels, display modes, resolution tables, VRAM hints
Save slot init (2639B) — allocates 32KB buffers per save, writes party snapshot

subsystems.notes: Uses param_1 (quest ID via in_ECX+0x1d4) to switch display mode (offsets 0x15a10-0x15a28, 0xc848=VRAM budget). Hardcoded resolution pairs: 800x500, 1280x960, 1920x1440 (although 1920x1440 hidden in UI per quirk §5). Sound thresholds at 0x159a0-0x159b0 (8000 Hz base, 48 kHz max). Save buffers allocated via operator_new(0x24 * 9 dwords).

subsystems.name: Menu/Input System

representative_vas.role: Input event poll (84B) — dequeues timed-out input events with 100ms window
Menu navigation handler (970B) — debounced up/down/left/right with wrapped loops
Option selection (795B) — highlights and commits menu choices

subsystems.notes: Input queue at in_ECX+0x108 (ring buffer, 64 entry slots). Timer fields at +0x1c/+0x20 use GetTickCount()+100/300ms waits. Menu state at in_ECX+0x1c (return 3/4 = highlight state). Wrapped navigation divides max by grid width (offset +0x3) and uses modulo for cycling. Quirk: 100ms debounce in case 4/6 gates repeat-fire.

subsystems.name: Character/Skill UI

representative_vas.role: Skill display init (1254B) — allocates skill list buffers, tracks element affinity (Fire/Water/Earth/Wind)
Skill animation setup (366B) — positions skill sprite groups on battle grid
Status effect icon renderer (144B) — draws poison/sleep/blind overlays

subsystems.notes: Skill elements stored as 0-3 enum (offset +0x7). Calls FUN_0040f3e0 to allocate glyph buffers (0x168 size per skill, stride 0x28). Uses DAT_008a7708 as sprite atlas base. Party slot structure (0x294 bytes) embeds skill list pointer at +0x3a. Port difficulty: element string hardcoding in switch at line 146-160 (Water/Earth strings at 0x8552fc / 0x8552f4).

subsystems.name: Save Game Manager

representative_vas.role: Save slot initialization (2639B)
Save data serializer (52B) — writes party checkpoint

subsystems.notes: Save buffer allocated with operator_new(0x24), initialized to zeros. Writes character name, level, HP, MP snapshots. Uses param_6 to select which save slot (0-7). No checksum visible in this band; deferred to 0x005a**** range. Offset +0x290 = slot completion marker.

quirks.title: Off-by-one in menu loop bound checking
quirks.desc: Menu navigation wraps using (count / cols) and (count % cols), but comparison is (index < count-1) at line 116. If grid_height==1 and index==0, wrapping down enters modulo-0 trap. Careful byte-verification needed before port.

quirks.title: 100ms debounce timer resets to 300ms on first press
quirks.desc: GetTickCount() + 300 on first trigger, then +100 on repeat. Not a bug, but non-obvious UX: slow first repeat, then fast auto-repeat. Mimics early arcade game feel.

quirks.title: Tilemap collision lookup uses (x&0xffff) as array index
quirks.desc: Clamping via (((int)uVar5 < 0) - 1 & uVar5) is technically sign-extend clamp, not wraparound. Safe in 32-bit, but unusual; suggests original code was ported from assembly or macro-heavy C++.

quirks.title: Scenario type IDs are sparse (0x111d4-0x111e8 range)
quirks.desc: Only 5 case IDs used out of possible 20. Large gaps in enum suggest abandoned branches or cut content. Matches typical game-dev lifecycle.

quirks.title: Save slot init uses operator_new(0x24) for 9 dwords but zero-fills with stride mismatches
quirks.desc: Allocates 36 bytes, clears 9*4-byte runs with mixed pointer/undefined4 writes. Loop at line 71 increments puVar5 by 1 (undefined4*), clearing 36 bytes exactly, but the stride confusion (mixed types) is a sign of sloppy refactoring.

maps.one_liner: Entity/sprite state machines with animation, collision, dialog, and action dispatch for game characters and NPCs.

subsystems.name: Entity frame / state machine core

representative_vas.role: Main entity per-frame update dispatcher - massive state switch on action code (0xc35a-0x1875d), manages animation timing, position, collisions, voice/dialog
Entity allocation and spatial placement - alloc 0xa500 entity arena (0x40 slots at stride 0x294), run collision checks, initialize collider mesh
Entity dtor - cleanup allocation of sub-collider heap pointers (array at +0x290)

subsystems.notes: Entities are pooled in a parent object at offset 0x40, each occupies 0x294 bytes. sub-colliders live at +0x290 (0x40 pointers to 0x50-byte objects). Entity has packed animation state: +0x48=animation ID, +0x58=animation phase, +0x5c=frame counter, +0x38=character type/class. Uses global DAT_008a9b50 for instance list and DAT_008a6e80 for engine state. This-call methods.

subsystems.name: Entity input validation and targeting

representative_vas.role: Predicate: can entity take action on target? checks if target slot active, alive, type-compatible (player/NPC mode), same team
Spatial collision detection - iterate all sub-colliders, test AABB against query box, support rotated sprite collision (player item array at +0x290)
Small checker - likely predicate for action viability (52 bytes)

subsystems.notes: Entity targeting lives in a global instance table (DAT_008a9b50+0x2788) with 0x11-encoded max entities. Collision mesh stored per entity, iterated for hit detection. Offsets suggest entity payload begins around +0x2c, sprite data around +0x40.

subsystems.name: Animation and sprite rendering

representative_vas.role: Sprite rendering helper - compute draw position from animation frame + entity pose, call FUN_005b15e0 (rendering layer), handle shadows/secondary pass
Sprite frame/pose lookup (692 B) - likely sprite atlas accessor
Animation phase update (91 B) - small increment/reset for anim counter
Scene-wide animation sync? (67 B)

subsystems.notes: Defers to external FUN_005b15e0 for actual rasterization. Sprite metadata in +0x2e0, +0x2e4, +0x2f4 offsets suggest frames, variant ID, extra param. Animation stored as packed array at +0x2c4 (cost?), +0x2c8 (offset?). Pose stored around +0xb0-0xb4.

subsystems.name: Character action handlers

representative_vas.role: Boss enemy action engine (2221 B) - handles case=5/6/7 (special monster attacks), manages cooldown, sound effects (FUN_00407ba0), pose transitions
NPC/minion action handler (2221 B) - large state machine, likely handles shop/dialog/follower behaviors
Treasure/chest/object interaction (3428 B) - case=3/5 (treasure), manages opening anim, particle effects, UI dialog trigger
Enemy group action (1338 B) - orchestrate multi-enemy behavior chains

subsystems.notes: Each handler is a large switch on entity.action_code (stored at +0x48). Action codes are 16-bit: 0xc35a-0x18752 observed. Return value indicates completion (0=done, nonzero=busy). Uses local state at +0x54, +0x58 to manage multi-frame sequences. Calls FUN_005bf505 for time queries.

subsystems.name: Dialog and voice trigger

representative_vas.role: Dialog line queue/playback (1015 B) - dispatch voice/sprite portrait, check player ready, return when speech done
Character dialog state handler (3869 B) - large dialog FSM with case=0x59/0x5a, manages portrait anim, waits for animation sync
Dialog trigger wrapper (89 B) - queue dialog for entity

subsystems.notes: Dialog references global table at in_ECX+0x15b54 (size at +0x15b5c). Dialog data at stride 0x510 per line. Uses DAT_0062e3c0, DAT_0062e558 for portrait/UI. Dialog FSM uses +0x58 for phase counter (0-5), +0x74 for sync flag.

subsystems.name: Damage, status effects, and item use

representative_vas.role: Character skill/ability dispatch (2552 B) - case=0x39/0x3a/0x3b/0x3c, execute damage calc, call FUN_00461240 (damage apply), trigger hit anim
Status effect applier (2523 B) - poison/burn/freeze, manage duration at +0x5c, visual indicators
Healing/buff handler (819 B) - apply recovery, trigger heal particle
Item consumption (751 B) - consume inventory item, apply effect

subsystems.notes: Damage values pulled from skill table and scaled by character stats. FUN_00461240 is the damage applier. Status effect duration stored at +0x5c. Effects reference global data: DAT_00651018, DAT_00651668, DAT_00651cb8, DAT_00652308 (skill/effect metadata).

subsystems.name: Pathfinding and movement

representative_vas.role: Movement speed calculator (424 B) - map character stat to pixel/frame velocity, account for terrain modifiers
Path segment interpolation (227 B) - lerp between waypoints
Pathfind initialization (150 B) - query map, seed path search

subsystems.notes: Movement state stored at +0x28 (position), +0x2c (direction/variant), +0x1c (speed). Uses FUN_005bf505 for frame delta time. Terrain/collision referenced from in_ECX+0x750 (scene manager).

subsystems.name: Special scripted actions

representative_vas.role: Cutscene/boss event orchestrator (1528 B) - boss-specific anim sequences, multi-phase logic for major encounters
Environmental hazard/trap trigger (2164 B) - spike, lava, ice effects
Party event/formation handler (1117 B) - manage player party idle poses, formation changes
Summon/spell effect dispatcher (505 B)

subsystems.notes: Heavily parameterized on action_code. Some actions require async particle/audio, queued through in_ECX+0xc7f4 (effect queue). Camera control via in_ECX+0x750. Uses spine-like bone data at +0x2e0.

quirks.title: Entity arena pool with inline sub-object allocation
quirks.desc: Entities live in a pre-allocated arena at in_ECX[0x40] (0xa500 bytes = space for ~0x40 entities at 0x294 stride). For each entity, allocation calls operator_new(0x50) 0x40 times for a collider sub-mesh (stored via ptrs at entity+0x290). This is a hand-rolled pool; no virtual dtor calls during entity kill (see 0x441270 which manually cleans up).

quirks.title: Action code as 16-bit enum multiplexing
quirks.desc: Entity action dispatch is a gigantic switch (lines 191-597) on in_ECX[0x75] (action code). Codes in range 0xc35a-0x1875d (up to ~6,000 different actions). Many handlers are separate FUN_ entries that take entity as param_1. Suggests this is a data-driven action interpreter; the code in 0x442a70 is the dispatcher, individual handlers are micro-VMs for each action type.

quirks.title: Animation frame timing via global lookup table
quirks.desc: Animation frame delay indexed from DAT_008a9358: `iVar8 = (&DAT_008a9358)[-((int)(iVar8 + (iVar8 >> 0x1f & 3U)) >> 2)]`. The negative subscript + shift pattern suggests a packed lookup or variable-width encoding, likely holding frame duration for each sprite sheet.

quirks.title: Multi-frame action sequence with phase FSM
quirks.desc: Actions split into phases (0-5) stored at +0x58. Example: cast spell (case 0x59) has phases: init anim -> wait for sync -> cast -> wait for hit -> cleanup. Frame counter at +0x5c increments within each phase. Some actions parameterized by +0x54 (subphase), +0x5a (limit counter). This is a coroutine-like pattern.

quirks.title: Collision mesh iterated per frame
quirks.desc: Every collision query loops through all sub-colliders at entity+0x290 (array of 0x40 pointers to 0x50-byte boxes). Each sub-collider stores 4 ints (AABB bounds). No spatial partitioning; O(n^2) for n entities with ~0x40 colliders each. Functional for 1-4 players + ~10 enemies.

quirks.title: Positional state split between entity and external pose object
quirks.desc: Entity stores action-relative offset at +0x28 (+0x2c for direction variant). Absolute position comes from param_8 (a sprite/drawable object passed in). Sprite object has position at +0x10, size at +0xc. This suggests entities are logic nodes, not rendering nodes; position decoupled from animation frame. Allows skeleton/bone animation to be computed elsewhere.

quirks.title: Action result codes (0=done, 1=busy) not boolean, used for chaining
quirks.desc: Many action handlers return int, used in if (iVar5 == 0) branches. Return 1 forces FUN_00467bb0 (likely action complete callback). This allows one-shot vs looping actions in the dispatcher.

quirks.title: Dialog tied to time via FUN_005bf505 millis query
quirks.desc: Dialog waits for character animation to finish (check +0x74), then checks in_ECX+0x15b54 (dialog line table). Uses time queries to timeout if anim hangs. No pre-baked dialog timing; tied to actual frame count / engine clock.

quirks.title: Velocity/speed scaling via skill metadata arrays
quirks.desc: Movement speed not hard-coded; pulled from skill/item table (param_8+0x2c4, stride varies). Then `(stat * param_9) / 1000` scales by effect multiplier. This allows enemy AI to vary speed, support items to grant speed buff.

maps.one_liner: Dialog/sprite animation state machines and game-entity rendering dispatch; the main render-per-frame bottleneck where sprite batches and visual effects are choreographed.

subsystems.name: Dialog / sprite animation state machine

representative_vas.role: Largest (19.4 KB): master dialog runner; orchestrates dialog sprite state transitions (0x2775/0x2778/0x277d IDs), animation frame advancement via FUN_00426f20 and FUN_004072c0, and calls FUN_00467500 to update animation pointers.
14 KB: entity action dispatcher; switch on entity type ID (0x2782/0x2783 common), calls FUN_0045db20 to advance animations, manages state counters in param_1+0x5c.
9.4 KB: dialogue sprite frame sequencer; handles sprite ID references at DAT_00636260/DAT_0063a008, increments animation state at param_1+0x5c, returns sprite pointers.
10.7 KB: render-frame dialog orchestrator; reads dialog state at (in_ECX + 0x40), reinitializes animation frames, delegates to system-time dependent frame calculation.
Animation state reset helper (185 B): atomically swaps sprite pointers at param_1+0x4c/0x50, increments frame counter DAT_008a9b50+0x27a0, guards state transitions.

subsystems.notes: Core class operates on param_1 (entity struct) with fixed offsets: 0x48=current_sprite_id, 0x56=animation_progress%, 0x58=frame_state, 0x5c=frame_timer, 0x6c=sprite_ptr, 0x74=animation_done_flag. Heavy use of jump-table dispatches on sprite IDs (0x2775, 0x2778, 0x277d, etc.). Global frame counter at DAT_008a9b50+0x27a0 wraps at 0xffffff. Thiscall convention confirmed by (in_ECX + offset) patterns. Port difficulty: MEDIUM (vtable recovery needed for 0x4072c0/0x426f20 sprite loaders).

subsystems.name: Game entity render dispatch

representative_vas.role: 8.7 KB: entity action ID dispatcher (case 0x2/0x12/etc); calls FUN_0044a160 for setup, drives monster/NPC state machines.
1.5 KB: in-game event runner; dispatches on sprite ID 0x21/0x22/0x23, calls FUN_00461240 to manage animation sequence.
3.2 KB: interactive sprite controller; dispatches on entity.type (0x13), calls FUN_00466930 to load sprite frames, calls FUN_00447900 to render.

subsystems.notes: Entities share param_1 struct format with dialog system (offsets 0x38/0x48/0x58/0x5c/0x6c reused). Heavy cross-calls to FUN_00407b80, FUN_00407ba0, FUN_005bf505 (timer reads). In-game entity list accessed via (in_ECX + 0x15xxx) offsets. Thiscall vtables inferred from in_ECX refs. Port difficulty: MEDIUM (entity list enumeration, sprite ID→address mapping).

subsystems.name: Sprite frame sequencing & animation

representative_vas.role: 3.4 KB: sprite frame collision checker; reads sprite layout at (in_ECX + 0x15950/0x15954), applies distance/orientation logic, manages sprite.0x20/0x24/0x28.
1.3 KB: sprite visual state updater; case on param_1+0x38 (entity type), reads global sprite tables (in_ECX + 0x159d4/0x159dc/0x159e0/0x159e4/0x159e8), updates entity.0x6c.
7.2 KB: sprite boundary culling for rendering batch; checks sprite bounds at (in_ECX + 0x3d0) against camera viewport, gates frame draw.

subsystems.notes: Shared offset access: entity.0x38=type, 0x28=Y_position, 0x20/0x24=X_position; sprite layout accessed via (in_ECX + 0x159xx) table array. Globals DAT_008a6e80 (main sprite context), in_ECX+0x750 (camera/scene ptr). Port difficulty: MEDIUM-HIGH (sprite table management, bounds arithmetic).

subsystems.name: Render-time dialogue sequence management

representative_vas.role: 4.7 KB: dialogue event sequencer; case on sprite ID 0x62/0x63/0x99, reads event state from (in_ECX + 0x15ad0/0x15ad4/0x15b54), manages DAT_0062c8a8 sprite pointers.
434 B: sprite animation slide/scroll controller; manages 4-stage animation (0x58 state machine), loads sprite from param_3+0x194/0x328/0x4bc offsets, calls FUN_0054db10 to apply movement.
237 B: audio/wave reserve handler; indexes wave bank at (in_ECX + 0x1b0) + 0x14 + param_1 * 0x24.

subsystems.notes: Dialogue sprite pointers live at param_3 (typically a sprite-metadata struct). State tracking at entity+0x58/0x5c. Movement deltas apply to entity+0x78. Global event state in in_ECX+0x15ad0 range (16 dwords). Port difficulty: HIGH (sprite metadata struct offsets, event ID→action mapping).

subsystems.name: Sprite batch render accumulation

representative_vas.role: 8.9 KB: sound/audio frame accumulator; reads audio state from (in_ECX + 0x590), pushes 0x200/0xd0/0xcb frame events onto a 0x40-entry ringbuffer at (in_ECX + 0x108).
772 B: sprite render call builder; computes sprite scale/offset via FUN_004481f0/FUN_00426e00, delegates to FUN_005b15e0 for final batch push (handles 3x camera-wrap cases).

subsystems.notes: Sprite ringbuffer at (in_ECX + 0x108), 0x40 entries, each 4 bytes. Frame accumulation checks camera bounds, applies scrolling offsets via (in_ECX + 0xc7e4/0xc7f4/0xc7f8). Port difficulty: HIGH (ringbuffer management, FUN_005b15e0 batch API is outside this range).

quirks.title: Frame counter wraps at 0xffffff, not power-of-2
quirks.desc: Global frame counter at DAT_008a9b50+0x27a0 increments per entity state change but resets to 0 (not wraps) when reaching 0xffffff. This is unusual — most engines use power-of-2 wraps. Suggests deliberate avoidance of arithmetic edge cases, or a quirk of the original sprite-scheduling logic.

quirks.title: Sprite ID dispatch uses exact values (0x2775, 0x2778, 0x277d), not ID ranges
quirks.desc: Dialog animation state machine switches on exact sprite IDs (0x2775, 0x2778, 0x277d) and resets all 10 state fields identically on each branch. No fallthrough; suggests hand-coded sprite ID table, not data-driven. Each ID gets its own reset block (40 bytes) — inefficient but explicit.

quirks.title: Animation progress tracked as percentage (0-0xd2) not frame count
quirks.desc: Entity+0x56 holds animation_progress as 0-210 (0xd2), not tick count. Line 13 of 0x467500 gates state transition on this percentage: `*(ushort *)(param_1 + 0x56) / 100 <= param_3 / 100`. This is a normalized-time check, suggests original engine used continuous time values normalized to 0-210, unusual for a discrete sprite sequencer.

quirks.title: Global sprite table at in_ECX+0x15d{c,d,d4,d8,e0,e4,e8} for entity types 1-4
quirks.desc: Entity type (0x38) determines which global sprite table to use: type=1 -> in_ECX+0x15980/0x15994, type=2 -> in_ECX+0x159dc/0x159e0/0x159e4, type=3 -> in_ECX+0x159d4. Tables are contiguous but the offset pattern is irregular (0x159d4, 0x159d8, 0x159dc, 0x159e0, 0x159e4, 0x159e8). Suggests a hand-rolled entity-type-dispatch, not a vtable.

quirks.title: Sprite pointer reset bundles 10 fields at once (0x48, 0x54, 0x56, 0x58, 0x5a, 0x5c, 0x78, 0x4c, 0x50, +0x64)
quirks.desc: When transitioning dialog sprites, the code resets 10 fields in lockstep: state IDs, timers, pointers, and flags. This is a symptom of a monolithic entity struct that conflates animation state, sprite pointers, and rendering flags — suggests the port will need a similar struct layout.

quirks.title: Movement deltas encoded as negative when entity.0x2c == 3 (direction flag)
quirks.desc: Sprite movement at param_1+0x78 is negated if entity+0x2c == 3 (likely a direction/facing flag). This is a common trick to avoid duplicating animation sequences for left/right sprites, but it's embedded in the rendering code rather than the animation loader — suggests the original engine had limited resource-deduplication.

quirks.title: Ringbuffer for sprite events at in_ECX+0x108, 0x40 entries, manual head rotation
quirks.desc: Sprite rendering events are queued into a ringbuffer with no index tracking — each push copies all prior entries up by one slot (lines 47-50, 68-72). This is O(n^2) in the batch size (max 64 entries). Suggests original engine didn't have a proper ringbuffer implementation.

maps.one_liner: Battle/combat state machine, NPC AI, UI text/dialog rendering, and sprite effect coordination.

subsystems.name: Battle State Machine / Combat Orchestration

representative_vas.role: 20KB master battle phase controller; dispatches on combat state (0x186a1-0x186a9 magic IDs); calls into all other subsystems per phase
1294B battle update loop; state machine that sequences NPC turn order, calculates damage, triggers SFX
3514B combat turn finalizer; resolves attack validity, applies buffs/debuffs, plays character reaction animations

subsystems.notes: Class-like structure in_ECX holds vtable at +0x75 (magic ID dispatch table at PTR_DAT_0056bfa4 pattern). Key globals: in_ECX+0x2c (turn counter), in_ECX+0x83 (active character object), in_ECX+0x8a/0x8b (hit count limits). Enum-heavy: 0x186a1-0x186a9 are magic move class IDs; *(short*)(param_2 + 0x58) tracks internal phase. No clear dtor; aggressive GetTickCount() time-gating (200ms, 500ms thresholds common).

subsystems.name: NPC AI / Attack Selection Logic

representative_vas.role: 7461B monster/NPC decision tree; evaluates arena position (0x2c=left/right), distance-weighted attack selection, teammate proximity influence
2364B move viability checker; validates attack context (case 0x9-0x12), calculates expected damage spread, applies post-hit bonus scaling
1120B ally distance calculator; sweeps 32 character slots (DAT_008a9b50 + 0x1160), ranks by proximity for spell targeting

subsystems.notes: Character state at param_1 + 0x1dc (type enum), param_2 + 0x28 (HP), param_2 + 0x2c (facing dir 1=left,3=right). Pathfinding: FUN_0047dbb0 called for arc queries (0x640=fixed arc width). Complex interaction: in_ECX + 0xc854/0xc858 = shield/barrier objects; *(int*)(in_ECX + 0x1596c) = arena half-width. Cost/benefit analysis cached in local arrays (local_b4[] = sprite indices, local_80[] = action queue). Port difficulty: MEDIUM (requires understanding struct layout of character objects).

subsystems.name: Sprite/Particle Rendering & Effects

representative_vas.role: 369B sprite render call wrapper; multiplexes to FUN_005b15e0 with offset/palette params; handles effect lifecycle (increments in_ECX + 0x22c counter)
198B rotation-to-delta converter; takes angle at param_1 + 0x34, applies sin/cos, writes X/Y deltas for smooth 360-deg movement
589B positioned sprite spawner; places sprite at offsets relative to character center, applies param-based SFX timing
326B atan2 + quadrant resolver; computes angle between two points, used for particle spray direction

subsystems.notes: Rendering pipeline: FUN_00476ef0 → FUN_005b15e0 (external, likely in GFX subsystem). Sprite heap at in_ECX + 0x1b0 (max slots *(ushort*)(in_ECX + 0x1b4)). Floating-point trig heavy (_DAT_008a9a38 = pi/1000 constant). Palette ramps in DAT_008a6ec4[]. Hard constraint: in_ECX + 0x228 = sprite-play-until counter; if hit, calls FUN_00556180(0) to commit render. Port difficulty: HIGH (depends on external sprite blitter, needs precise parameter mapping).

subsystems.name: Audio/Wave Coordination

representative_vas.role: 509B wave slot allocator; reserves audio channel from pool at in_ECX + 0x1b0, calls FUN_0040bb90 to queue sample, updates SFX playback counter
Calls FUN_005bb870/FUN_005bb8c0 extensively to sync audio with animation frames (wave-to-sprite binding)

subsystems.notes: Wave pool: 32-slot table at DAT_008a9b50 + 0x1160 (4-byte entries, character ptrs). Reservation check: *(ushort*)(in_ECX + 0x1b4) must not overflow. SFX bytes at offset +0x20 into pool entry. Globals: DAT_008a6e80 (likely audio device handle), in_ECX + 0x6c/0x6d (wave reservation flags). Port difficulty: MEDIUM (defer to existing audio subsystem; mapping is mostly parameter routing).

subsystems.name: UI Text/Dialog Rendering

representative_vas.role: 727B dialog box renderer; writes animation state to param_1 + 0x58 (0→1→final), updates position in_ECX + 0x22c
144B text color palette writer; applies ramp based on in_ECX color index
230B popup damage/heal number animator; fades and scrolls numeric text

subsystems.notes: Dialog lifetime: param_1 + 0x28 (age in ms, 0-40000), incremented by 2000/frame. Position: param_1 + 4/8 (X/Y). Palette ID at in_ECX + 0x3d/0x3e. Text anchor: *(short*)(param_2 + 0x48) flags mirroring. Port difficulty: MEDIUM (mostly coordinate transforms; text rendering likely deferred to GFX system).

subsystems.name: Update Tick Dispatcher

representative_vas.role: Polls all effect/animation counters (in_ECX + 0x14200-range), decrements lifetimes, prunes expired objects
Aggregate of character state machine; ticks HP regeneration, buffs, status effects

subsystems.notes: Frame boundary: GetTickCount() at entry, compared against per-object last-update time stamps. Decrement patterns: *(short*)(in_ECX + 0x14804) -= 1, *(short*)(in_ECX + 0x14248) -= 1 (per-character cooldowns). Early-exit chains common (if X return;). Port difficulty: LOW (mostly housekeeping).

quirks.title: Off-by-one in NPC attack cost lookup
quirks.desc: Comparison `((*(int*)(param_2 + 0x28) <= *(int*)(in_ECX + 0x1596c)) - 1 & 0xc80) + 0xc80` uses decrement-and-mask to clamp; this is a sign-extension trick to convert bool to 0/-1, then add offset. Works but fragile if const 0xc80 changes (arena width).

quirks.title: Wrapped modulo in turn counter update
quirks.desc: In state 3, `(iVar4 + 1) % 0x28` computes next valid attacker; hardcoded 40 (0x28) implies max 40 characters per scene. Off-by-one risk if iVar4 == 39.

quirks.title: Implicit magic ID enum (0x186a1-0x186a9)
quirks.desc: Switch on in_ECX[0x75] dispatches magic move class 0x186a1 (fireball) through 0x186a9 (special?). No extern enum definition in decompile; likely hand-coded magic table in retail. Port must preserve these IDs.

quirks.title: Sprite lifecycle via counter, not lifetime field
quirks.desc: Sprite rendering driven by in_ECX + 0x22c counter; when >= in_ECX + 0x228 limit, sprite is freed. No per-sprite TTL; instead, all sprites tied to single global counter. Means sprites must be queued in order or they'll be culled early.

quirks.title: Angle wrapping via atan2 + quadrant case
quirks.desc: Hand-rolled atan2 with 4-quadrant correction; adds/subtracts _DAT_008a6b28/0x808... global constants (likely pi/pi/2 in rad*1000). Risk: floating-point precision drift if constants are approximations.

quirks.title: Circular position update with brute-force sin/cos per frame
quirks.desc: Every frame: sin(angle)*const, cos(angle)*const, then __ftol to convert. No pre-computed table; floating-point overhead per sprite. Potential perf bottleneck for many simultaneous effects.

quirks.title: GetTickCount() time-gating thresholds magic numbers
quirks.desc: Comparison `uVar7 < DVar5 - iVar6` with hardcoded 10ms, 50ms, 200ms thresholds scattered throughout. No abstraction; will break if frame-rate changes. Port must parameterize these or risk off-by-one frame timing bugs.

quirks.title: Uninitialized conditional branches in dialog state machine
quirks.desc: Goto LAB_004780d7 from multiple branches without init of local_4; if path taken, local_4 may be garbage. Compiler likely eliminated dead code; should be safe but fragile.

maps.one_liner: Animation/sprite rendering, collision detection, and UI layer (text, line drawing) implementation with character state and effect management.

subsystems.name: Character Animation & Sprite Rendering

representative_vas.role: Main character animation sequencer - 8605 bytes, large switch on animation types (0x56b4), computes sprite position/bounds with in_ECX offset chaining
Secondary animation handler - 5082 bytes, handles effect/status animation with state transitions, character flip logic (facing direction)
Combat/ability animation - 4593 bytes, manages animated effect overlays and timing
Visual effect arc/circle rendering - 4389 bytes, draws animated arcs using GDI Arc() API with scaling and rotation

subsystems.notes: Sprite struct offset 0x294 per sprite index; animation state at in_ECX[0x56b4], 0x56ad, 0x56ba, 0x56af, 0x56b0. Uses frame timing with FUN_005bf505() for pseudo-random jitter. Global sprite data at in_ECX[0x10] with vtable-like access (in_ECX[0x10] + frame_idx * 0x294). Large local arrays (512 bytes) suggest decompressed/transformed frame buffers. Port difficulty: MEDIUM - struct offsets load-bearing but obtainable from vtable recovery.

subsystems.name: Collision Detection & Hit Testing

representative_vas.role: AABB hit detection - 577 bytes, tests point-in-rectangle with offset arrays (stride 0x294), handles collision groups
Damage/knockback calculation - 1430 bytes, computes velocity magnitude & direction from attacker stats (in_ECX[0x750] + offsets 0xd8, 0xe0, 0xe4 = damage tiers)
Attack animation validator - 1932 bytes, checks if attack frame is valid for hit confirmation, uses FUN_0047fd40 for frame lookup

subsystems.notes: Collision groups stored at DAT_008a760c with vtable indirection. Damage calculation: stats at in_ECX[0x750] + {0x60,0x80,0xa0} for three damage types, scaled by ability multiplier (param_4). Hit frame detection uses parameter frame index vs. animation state. Port difficulty: MEDIUM - needs character vtable + stat block mapping.

subsystems.name: UI Text & Glyph Rendering

representative_vas.role: Glyph blit to HDC - 1221 bytes, renders individual glyphs from glyph table, color lookup from palette (in_ECX[0x174] = glyph vtable)
Line/path drawing - 641 bytes, draws polylines with angle calculation using atan2 and sin/cos, uses POINT array input
Text layout helper - 131 bytes, splits text into 40-char lines, marks spaces with 0xff
Dialog text rendering - 1672 bytes, iterates glyph table, handles text color + palette mode swapping

subsystems.notes: Glyph table: in_ECX[0x174] is parent struct, in_ECX[0x178] = array of glyph coords/advance, in_ECX[0x17c] = glyph frames. Line drawing uses angle as index into sin/cos tables (0x168 entries). Text color at in_ECX[0x1ac]. Port difficulty: MEDIUM - Win32 HDC coupling, but glyph pipeline is clean.

subsystems.name: Entity State & Movement Physics

representative_vas.role: Character state machine - 1874 bytes, updates position/velocity, handles status effects (paralysis, stun), uses in_ECX[0x14818] for character vtable
Screen boundary clamping - 844 bytes, keeps entity on-screen, uses in_ECX[0xc880] for screen state
Terrain/obstacle collision - 668 bytes, checks collision with tilemap (0xc80 = tile size), uses piVar6[0x2949] and [0x294a] as tile coords
Character animation event trigger - 1144 bytes, checks if character can perform action based on current animation frame, loops through frame array at in_ECX[0xca28]

subsystems.notes: Character vtable at in_ECX (this-call style). Position at in_ECX[0x750] (main stats block, stride 0x14 for multiple axes). Status effect counters at in_ECX[0x14802], [0x1428a-c], [0x14828]. Tilemap cells 0xc80x0xc80 pixels. Port difficulty: MEDIUM-HIGH - in_ECX chain complex, requires full class layout.

subsystems.name: Sound Effect & Audio Trigger

representative_vas.role: SFX playback - 818 bytes, looks up sound ID in DAT_008a6ec4 (sound table), calculates stereo pan from character position, calls into audio library
Particle/effect queue - 1289 bytes, manages queued particle effects, uses DAT_008a704c as effect pool

subsystems.notes: Sound table DAT_008a6ec4 indexed by sound_id & 0xffff; each entry is 8-byte slot with flags at +0, +4. Pan calculation: distance-based attenuation with thresholds 8000, 16000 at line 69-71 of 489280.c. Effect queue is ring buffer with head at local_28c & 0xffff. Port difficulty: HIGH - audio library call boundary unclear, likely proprietary ZDI wrapper.

subsystems.name: Combat Effect & Ability Animation

representative_vas.role: Ability effect initializer - 1374 bytes, spawns particle/visual effects for spells, clears screen buffer (0x1400 int32s = 5.5 KB), calls FUN_00490cd0 and FUN_00491ae0 for specific ability types
Attack collision checker - 1062 bytes, determines which enemies are hit by attack, uses bounding box overlap
Enemy knockback applicator - ~1000 bytes, applies impulse to enemy character after hit (not read but referenced heavily)

subsystems.notes: Effect buffer at DAT_008a9b50[0x1048] + 0x190030 (cleared at initialization). Ability database at DAT_008a9b50 with vtable lookups. Port difficulty: HIGH - ability parameter encoding unclear, effect system is data-driven.

quirks.title: Sprite struct stride is 0x294 (660 bytes), not power-of-2
quirks.desc: Multiple functions index arrays with stride 0x294 per sprite: in_ECX[0x10] + frame_idx * 0x294. This suggests the original class uses a dense packing layout rather than standard alignment. At 2KB per character (3x660 offsets) this is likely multiple characters sharing a contiguous buffer. Decompiler bug risk: offset calculation could be misread as 0x290 or 0x298.

quirks.title: Tile collision uses 0xc80 (3200 decimal) as tile size constant
quirks.desc: Terrain grid is indexed by dividing coordinates by 0xc80. This is 3200 pixels per tile, which is enormous for a 2D action RPG. Likely a legacy debug setting or the value is misread by decompiler. 0xc00 (3072) or 0x100 (256) would be more sensible. Verify in binary.

quirks.title: Animation type switch has 18 cases (0-0x11) with complex fallthrough
quirks.desc: Switch(in_ECX[0x56b4]) handles attack/ability animations, but cases 0x11 and 2 share logic (fallthrough). This is either intentional combo logic or evidence of sloppy refactoring. Case 0x11 sets local_4b8 = 0x4, then falls to case 2 which overwrites with local_4a0[1] = 0x75. Suggests two different animations can trigger the same sprite.

quirks.title: Pseudo-random frame jitter via FUN_005bf505() called per-animation
quirks.desc: Instead of seeding once per frame, animation sequencer calls FUN_005bf505() multiple times per render (once per case branch in the switch). This creates non-deterministic animation timing that varies by frame budget. Not necessarily a bug but unusual for frame-locked games; suggests either frame-skipping tolerance or animation preview code.

quirks.title: Stereo pan calculation uses hardcoded distance thresholds without normalization
quirks.desc: Sound pan calculation uses fixed thresholds: if distance < 8000, full pan left; if distance > 16000, full pan right; else interpolate. No distance-to-db conversion, just raw distance. This means pan changes discontinuously if camera moves (distance thresholds are in world units, not screen units). Likely intended for 2D topdown game where camera is always centered on player.

quirks.title: Text layout function uses strlen in a loop for line-breaking
quirks.desc: For each line, FUN_0048b850 recalculates string length with manual strlen instead of caching. Called per-line, so O(n^2) on text length. Minor inefficiency but suggests code written for short dialogs or copied from simpler version.

maps.one_liner: UI render dispatch and sprite/animation batching: tile/sprite rendering, character combat animations, shop/battle UI dialogs.

subsystems.name: Tile & sprite rendering (map/scene)

representative_vas.role: FUN_00490f30 - Main tile/sprite grid renderer (2002B); iterates map bounds, applies palette corrections, blits sprites to DDraw surfaces; core call site for sprite_slot.entries
Text/font renderer for UI labels (696B); measures/renders glyph sequences with width/height calculation
Sprite queue pusher (118B); enqueues rendered sprite to frame buffer, 0x3c stride
Animation frame fetch (129B); looks up sprite animation keyframe dimensions from offset 0x34 (stride 0x18)
Tile collision setter (206B); marks collision map bits (0x190030 + in_ECX base, strides 0x80 x 0xa0) for sprite spatial queries

subsystems.notes: Manages ar_sprite_slot objects (globally indexed, entries->a/b/c fields for bitmap/palette/refcount). Tile map encoded with grid stride 0xc80 (game units to pixel), collision bits packed in 32-bit rows. Port difficulty: moderate - requires exact struct offsets for sprite_slot and tile collision memory layout; palette application loops are unrolled switches (DAT_008a93fc color modes 0-5: neutral/sepia/red/blue/etc).

subsystems.name: Character spell/effect rendering (battle animations)

representative_vas.role: Spell cast visual renderer (3014B); character stat lookup (0x10+idx*0x294), spell particle emission (FUN_0044d160 projectile fetch), UI text push (FUN_00492670)
Character animation sequence player (2599B); scene entity anim controller w/ rotation (0xf0 rate), frame advance, damage number spawning
Animation key frame batch renderer (740B); loops animation frames by sprite ID + timing, calls FUN_004925e0 for dims + FUN_00492670 to emit
Vertical terrain/wall sprite handler (429B); renders tall sprites (0x1900 stride) with vertical collision bits
Character state sprite lookup (139B)

subsystems.notes: Character objects stored at [in_ECX+0x10 + idx*0x294]; animation ID field 0x48 (sprite index), 0x5c (duration), 0x66 (frame count). Heavy reuse of FUN_00417c40 (sprite keyframe lookup by animation+frame), FUN_00492670 (queue push). Multiple off-by-one patterns in frame iteration (0x48 state machine enum matches hardcoded spell ID table). Port difficulty: high - requires accurate character struct layout at stride 0x294; sprite lookup tables (0x760c globals) must be initialized.

subsystems.name: UI rendering (menus, dialogs, HUD)

representative_vas.role: Battle status UI renderer (3904B); damage numbers, item displays, character portraits; calls FUN_00498680/FUN_00498820 (stat text), FUN_0043e250 (text blit)
Shop/dialogue menu handler (5270B); item purchase/NPC talk state machine (0x1d8 cost calcs, 0x27c/0x280 inventory ptrs); emits shop window sprite frames
Dialogue/choice menu renderer (918B); text layout with wrapping
HUD element blit (394B)
Number-to-string for damage/stats (82B)

subsystems.notes: Game state 0x1d8 (cost), 0x27c/0x280 (inventory), 0x34 (text table). Menu navigation via keyboard input polling (FUN_00412db0 - input state 0..7). HUD driven by scene enum (0x1d4 scene ID). Port difficulty: high - scene state machines are dense switches with many branches per ID; string formatting hardcoded (s__s____d_008554e8 format string); requires complete game mode enum mapping.

subsystems.name: Animation key/sprite table lookups

representative_vas.role: Sprite frame lookup (106B); reads animation ID + frame from table, returns sprite vtable ptr
Referenced frequently; appears to be global sprite key table accessor
Animation table construction

subsystems.notes: Sprite tables indexed by enum (0..0x1ff approx). Animation frames are small structs (0x3c bytes each in queue buffers, 0x18-byte stride in animation def table at offset 0x34). Table base often fetched from in_ECX+0x34. Port difficulty: moderate - mostly leaf accessors, but require asset table initialization.

subsystems.name: Graphics mode/palette management

representative_vas.role: Palette setup helper (59B)
GDI polyline draw for debug/editor (641B); Bresenham rotation to angle
Palette blend/ramp (348B)

subsystems.notes: Global DAT_008a93fc holds color mode (0-5 enum: normal/sepia/red/blue/etc). Palette tables DAT_008a92b8/DAT_008a9304 (brightness ramps indexed by scene ID). Port difficulty: low - mostly utility functions; depends on correct color-mode enum values.

quirks.title: Sprite queue stride is 0x3c, collision map strides are (0x80, 0xa0)
quirks.desc: Sprite enqueue writes to a circular buffer at [ptr + count*0x3c]; count field at +0/+2 (ushort). Collision grid marks at offset 0x190030+in_ECX with coordinate packing: Y-major 0xa0-byte rows, X-major 0x80-byte inner stride. Mismatch would break spatial queries.

quirks.title: Animation frame ID lookup always precedes sprite enqueue
quirks.desc: Pattern: FUN_00417c40(anim_id, frame_num) → sprite_ptr, then immediately FUN_00492670(queue, sprite_ptr, x, y, ...). No alternate path for invalid sprite ID; missing table entries will cause null-ptr dereferences in downstream blit.

quirks.title: Character entity stride is 0x294 (660 bytes); hard-coded offset constants
quirks.desc: Every character access does [base + idx*0x294]; offsets 0x04 (x), 0x08 (y), 0x40 (width), 0x44 (height), 0x48 (sprite state enum), 0x5c (duration), 0x66 (frame count), 0x2c (rotation mode 0-3). Single misaligned offset breaks entire character renderer.

quirks.title: Color mode DAT_008a93fc = 0-5 enum drives palette application in 0x490f30 loop
quirks.desc: Switch on DAT_008a93fc (mode 0-5) calls FUN_004182d0(dst_pal, src_pal, R, G, B, 0) with fixed RGB triplets per mode. Mode 3 and 4 share branches; mode 5 sets all channels to 0x55. Mismatch in enum values will corrupt rendered colors.

quirks.title: Tile map collision is indexed by divide-by-0x20 (quant), strides use multiply-by-0x80/0xa0
quirks.desc: Collision check: iX = (param_3 + sign-extend-and-shift) >> 5; iY = (param_4 + sign-extend-and-shift) >> 5; bounds check [0..2c1030) x [0..2c1034); bit addr = iY*0x80 + iX*0xa0 + 0x190030 + in_ECX. Arithmetic errors (sign extension, stride order) will index wrong cells.

quirks.title: ar_sprite_slot::FUN_00491770 assumes exactly 256-dword palette (0x400 bytes)
quirks.desc: Loops iVar2=0x100 times, copying 4-byte chunks. Palette allocation is unconditional in entries->b. No bounds check; palette overrun will corrupt heap.

quirks.title: Shop menu state is encoded in dense switch on scene ID (0xc867ee6, 0xc867ee7, etc.)
quirks.desc: Each shop/NPC has a unique 32-bit ID; missing ID in switch falls through to default, skipping item dialog entirely. Port must have scene ID enum table matching retail behavior.

maps.one_liner: Game scene event dispatcher and party state machine: 77 functions orchestrating narrative branching, battle setup, character inventory, and turn-based combat.

subsystems.name: Scene event dispatch (narrative scripting VM)

representative_vas.role: Scene state 0x300 handler (28 KB, largest in band)
Scene state 0xf0 handler with battle loop (21.5 KB)
Scene state 0xd2 handler (20.7 KB)
Scene state 0x10e handler (15.5 KB)
Scene state 0xe6/0x382de handler (14.9 KB)

subsystems.notes: Vtable dispatch likely at DAT_008a9b50 + 0x1038; each function is a __thiscall handler that checks (scene_state[1] << 16 | scene_state[0]). Functions read from *(in_ECX + 0x14) for sub-state (0=init, 2=play, 3=event), branch on *(in_ECX + 4) + 0x1d8 (entity/object ID hash from FUN_00556eb0). Heavy use of FUN_00439680() as frame-blocker (returns 6 to exit). Calls back to FUN_00402f20, FUN_00402060, FUN_00402d40, FUN_00406210 for camera/cinematic setup. Pattern: setup locals, loop, dispatch on object ID, call narrative/animation helpers, pump frame, return. **Port difficulty: HIGH** — requires full decompile of dialogue/animation helper subsystem.

subsystems.name: Party/character state and inventory

representative_vas.role: Party member selection and swap (536 B) — iterate 8 slots, test status
Spawn/init party member in world (508 B)
Party sprite/animation selector by map state (453 B)
Party member state reset (304 B) — 30+ offset writes

subsystems.notes: Operates on in_ECX + 0x4030 (party array, 8 slots). 0x4cc250 does round-robin iteration (uVar5 < 8 check). Offset 0x9f0 is entity ID, 0x9c4 is type (1=hero, 2=npc), 0x27c is entity-class ID (0x5f5e168 reserved). FUN_004cc470 zeroes 20+ uint16/uint32 state fields starting at +0x14xxx offsets (skill cooldowns, buffs, debuffs). FUN_004cc820 allocates in round-robin and calls FUN_00426fd0, FUN_004c6160 for animation setup. Accesses global DAT_006c0cd0 (enemy database, stride 0xb8, field +0xae = ID, +0xaf = active). **Port difficulty: MEDIUM** — struct offsets well-defined but party system depends on battle physics.

subsystems.name: Battle orchestration and UI

representative_vas.role: Inn/shop handler (4.3 KB) — calls FUN_0041c390 (menu UI)
Puzzle/lock handler with numeric input (3.4 KB)
Battle prep scene (1020 B)

subsystems.notes: FUN_0041c390 is a UI menu dispatcher (takes strings for purchase/save/sell). FUN_004c45a0 runs a loop calling FUN_0041a2a0 for player input, FUN_005bf3ee for string formatting, stores result in local_13c. Checks *(in_ECX + 0x15f) for input state flags, uses FUN_00439680 as frame pump. Pattern mirrors scene dispatch: acquire lock, pump frame, check exit code (6=skip, 0xe=user input). **Port difficulty: MEDIUM-HIGH** — UI depends on dialog string table and input handling; puzzle logic straightforward.

subsystems.name: Scene state transition / sub-dispatch

representative_vas.role: Scene state 0x136 (5004 B)
Nighttime camp scene state 0xeb/0x39670 (2.6 KB)
Scene state 0x400 (1.7 KB)

subsystems.notes: Smaller state handlers that dispatch on object ID (FUN_00556eb0 hash to *(in_ECX + 4) + 0x1d8). FUN_004ccc90 enters at in_ECX[5]==0, reads *(in_ECX + 4) for object ID, calls FUN_004467d0 for anim update. Common pattern: query state at **state**[5] (phase counter), check completion with FUN_00439680, advance to next phase via in_ECX[8] = 6. **Port difficulty: MEDIUM** — mostly orchestration, depends on helper VMs for anim/dialog.

subsystems.name: Resource/object query helpers (sparse, merged with dispatchers)

representative_vas.role: Fountain event (1057 B) — iterates *(in_ECX + 8) + 0x40ac for objects
Object spawn allocator (706 B) — finds empty slot in 0x7804-sized array at +0x1804
Door/permission scene (1244 B)

subsystems.notes: FUN_004a5fa0 loops *(in_ECX + 8) + 0x4030 (party array), accesses +0x9f0 (entity ID), +0x9c4 (type), +0xa64/+0xa6c (accumulated stats). FUN_004a63d0 allocates in *(in_ECX) + 0x1804 array, size *(in_ECX) + 0x7804, caps at 0x800. Calls GetLastError, FormatMessageA if full (crash on overflow). **Port difficulty: LOW-MEDIUM** — array management standard, quota 0x800 is likely a quirk (see engine-quirks.md for capacity crash handler).

quirks.title: Object perpetuity state overflow at 0x800 crashes with debug log
quirks.desc: When the object spawn pool (*(in_ECX) + 0x1804, capacity *(in_ECX) + 0x7804) reaches 0x800 entries, the allocator calls GetLastError, formats the message with FormatMessageA, and logs it to OutputDebugString before crash. DAT_008a9534 is used as error buffer. String literal: 'The object perpetuity state area has been fully used'. This is a hard limit, not a realloc — suggests pooling was runtime tuned. Seen in FUN_004a63d0 (line 35) and FUN_004b3b20 (line 61).

quirks.title: DAT_008a9b50 vtable/manager singleton with 0x1038 scene state
quirks.desc: Every scene handler in this band reads *(DAT_008a9b50 + 0x1038) expecting a pointer to scene state. Scene state[0] is usually a two-byte map/area ID; state[1] is a screen/mode ID (0xd2, 0xf0, 0x136, 0x300, etc.). The second dereference reads state[4] for event-specific context (e.g. player type, sub-mode). This is a hard-coded global singleton manager, not passed via ctor. Pattern: *(int *)(*(int *)(DAT_008a9b50 + 0x1038) + 4) checks screen type. **Load-bearing architectural choice**: port must preserve this singleton pattern or thread-wrap it.

quirks.title: FUN_00439680 is the universal frame pump and exit blocker
quirks.desc: Every scene handler loops: set state, call FUN_00439680, check return for 6 (user requested skip/quit). Return 6 unwinds the entire scene. This is likely a frame-sync call that polls input, runs rendering, and returns 6 on ESC/quit. Called hundreds of times per scene handler. **Port critical path**: FUN_00439680 must yield to event loop and check Windows message pump; blocking port will hang.

quirks.title: FUN_00556eb0 is string ID → resource pointer hash resolver
quirks.desc: Patterns like `FUN_00556eb0(0x5f5e165)` appear everywhere. These are hardcoded hashes (CRC32-like, 32-bit). Same hash ID repeats across multiple handlers, suggesting global asset/entity directory. Example: 0x5f5e165, 0x5f5e166, 0x5f5e167, 0x5f5e168 are distinct hero/NPC references used in multiple maps. Port must pre-compute or cache these hashes.

quirks.title: Sub-state machine via in_ECX[5] phase counter
quirks.desc: Large handlers read in_ECX[5] (offset +0x14 from ECX) to track phase (0=init, 1=loop, 2=outro). Loop body branches on phase, advances with in_ECX[5]++. FUN_004ccc90 uses in_ECX[5]==0 guard at entry. FUN_004cd6d0 switches on *in_ECX[5]. This is re-entrant per frame: state persists in object, not on stack.

quirks.title: Party/character array is 8-slot fixed (0x4030, stride 1 int*)
quirks.desc: All party iteration assumes ushort uVar5 < 8 hardcoded limit. Accessed as *(in_ECX + 0x4030 + (uVar5 + uVar4 & 0x80000007) * 4). The mask 0x80000007 is a quirk—on x86 with sign extension, it wraps modulo 8 even if uVar5 overflows. Party size is runtime-fixed at 8, not variable.

maps.one_liner: Narrative scripting engine for game scenes — dialogue/cutscene sequencing and character state orchestration, predominantly event-driven FSM callbacks.

subsystems.name: Scene/event script system (narrative FSM)

representative_vas.role: prologue/tutorial classroom scene setup and dialogue branching
adventurer's guild quest/world initialization event chain
home first-floor dungeon exploration tutorial and mechanic discovery
gateball sports minigame tournament bracket state machine
dungeon encounter and combat progression event handler
late-game romantic/story divergence branching (Arche + Stella)

subsystems.notes: Each large function (8-20KB) is a coroutine-style scene entry point keyed off DAT_008a9b50+0x1038 (event type/ID pair). Functions check state via in_ECX[5] (scene phase counter), load assets via FUN_00556eb0 (pointer registry), sequence dialogue via FUN_0049d6e0, and poll FUN_00439680 (scene wait/pump) to suspend. Uses call/jump table at 0x5f5e165+ for character/NPC instances (signed asset refs like 0x5f5e165, 0x5f5e166). Nested switch blocks on quest/flag IDs (0x334be, 0x334c8, 0x334dc, 0x334dd, 0x334e6, 0x30db3, 0x30db8, 0x30dc2, 0x30dcc, 0x30dd6, 0x30de0) branch dialogue/sequence trees. Per-function lifecycle: init (phase=0) → scene pump loop → transition/cleanup → return 0 (next scene) or return 6 (abort).

subsystems.name: Character state & party inventory management

representative_vas.role: summon party member into battle, search free slot in DAT_008a9b50+0x1160 (party array)
set character status flags (offset +0x9c4=1 active/2 idle, +0x9f4=parameter)
flush character status transitions and trigger state callbacks (loop over in_ECX+0x4030 party slots)

subsystems.notes: Party is 8-slot array at in_ECX+0x4030 (each slot → int* to character object). Each character has: offset 0x9c4 (state: 1=active, 2=idle), 0x9c8 (inventory count), 0x9cc (inventory array), 0x9f0 (character ID tag), 0x9f4 (param). Inventory scanning loop at in_ECX+0x2014 (stride 0x10, max 0x20 items). DAT_008a9b50+0x1160 is 32-slot monster/character pool (round-robin iterator at in_ECX+0xcc). Mid-range difficulty — opaque struct layout, relies on byte-verified offsets.

subsystems.name: Dungeon/encounter driver (battle scene setup)

representative_vas.role: dungeon floor layout/monster spawn choreography and intro dialogue
boss battle special-case routing and elemental stone loot logic

subsystems.notes: Interleaves FUN_0041f0e0 (asset load) and FUN_004cbf80 (character preset assignment) to configure NPC/enemy instances before FUN_00439680 pump. Checks DAT_008a9b50+0x1038[0/1] for dungeon type ID (e.g. 0x382fc, 0x334c8). Uses magical item hashes (0x35a4e90x constants) as asset/elemental stone registry keys. Difficulty: high — requires cross-reference with battle/physics engine for damage formulas and turn sequencing.

subsystems.name: Quest/item state persistence

representative_vas.role: item status update trigger on character active→idle transition

subsystems.notes: Item slots are at in_ECX+0x2014, 0x20 max. Each item has ID at +0x0, state at +0x4. FUN_005b4a10 is called on item use. No dedicated inventory struct — items are packed as 16-byte records in character object. Medium difficulty — inventory model is functional but tightly coupled.

quirks.title: Dialogue state via in_ECX[5] phase counter
quirks.desc: Every large scene function uses in_ECX[5] as a 0-indexed resumption point counter within that scene. On first entry (phase=0), initialize; on subsequent entries, continue from where pump returned. This is a degenerate coroutine pattern — no actual continuation state is saved, just a counter that controls which if() branch to enter next. Very fragile to edit.

quirks.title: Asset registry via magic hash keys (0x35a4e90x, 0x5f5e1xx)
quirks.desc: Character instances, items, and NPCs are looked up via hardcoded hex tokens (0x35a4e902 = Sophia teacher, 0x5f5e165 = main PC Arche, 0x5f5e166 = companion Sana). These are presumably hash-based lookups into a global asset table, but the hash function is opaque. Porting requires maintaining this registry.

quirks.title: Synchronous scene pump with early-return for abort (return 6)
quirks.desc: Every FUN_00439680() call is checked for return value 6, which signals 'user cancelled / quit'. If detected, entire scene function returns 6 immediately. This is a simple but inelegant way to propagate cancellation — no exception mechanism, no cleanup. Implies all resources are auto-cleaned (no explicit dtor calls visible).

quirks.title: Character ID tagging via offset +0x27c (not 0x9f0)
quirks.desc: Two different offsets used for character ID: 0x4ee490 uses +0x27c, 0x4e6030 uses +0x9f0. Both observed in same band. Suggests either: (a) multiple character object types with different layouts, or (b) decompiler confusion. High risk for porting.

quirks.title: Quest branch via global state flag DAT_008a9b50+0x1038
quirks.desc: Event script routing is controlled entirely by checking a single global pair (DAT_008a9b50+0x1038)[0] and [1]. This is a 2-word opaque FSM state. No scene-specific save logic visible — all persistence must live in DAT_008a9b50 memory region, implying a monolithic global game state object.

maps.one_liner: Gameplay core: scene dispatcher, character rendering, collision/physics, enemy spawning, and cutscene orchestration.

subsystems.name: Cutscene/Scene Dispatcher

representative_vas.role: Primary game scene driver (22KB) with 100+ scene IDs dispatching cutscene sequences
Secondary scene handler (21KB) with animation/dialogue/effect orchestration
Small scene entry point (76B) validating scene context before dispatch

subsystems.notes: Both dispatchers are monolithic switch-statement on scene IDs (0x186ca-0x186d9, 0x186a1-0x186b0 range). Heavy nesting with game state checks. Likely loads and drives all cutscenes, NPC interactions, and story progression. Hardcoded scene sequences suggest story data is embedded or asset-driven. Class pattern: uses in_ECX offset access (0x10, 0x8a, 0x97, etc.) indicating thiscall instance methods. No separate AST—just imperative sequences.

subsystems.name: Sprite/Character Rendering

representative_vas.role: Main sprite copy/animate (11.6KB) handling frame buffers and animation cycles
Small sprite wrapper (60B) redirecting to graphics backend

subsystems.notes: 0x54f980 performs large monolithic sprite buffer copies (0x284-0x288 byte offsets) with field-by-field struct updates. Handles animation frame state, z-order, rotation. Accesses frame data at +0x40, +0x44 offsets via this-call. Likely C++ class method. Call site references: FUN_00445d30 (graphics update), FUN_00442460 (frame selection).

subsystems.name: Tile/Map Collision System

representative_vas.role: Main collision processor (812B) checking walkability and obstacles
Large collision calc (792B) with coordinate transforms and tile validation
Collision data unpacking (1555B) with modular arithmetic on tile coords
Line-of-sight or path trace (945B) iterating through tile boundaries
Tile mark function (214B) setting collision bits in grid

subsystems.notes: Grid-based collision: map stored as 2D array of 0xc80 (3200 decimal) tile units. Accessed via offset formula (row * 0x80 + col) * 0x10. Accessed at in_ECX + 0x14003c. Stride 0x10 suggests 16-byte tile struct (flags, properties, etc.). Heavy use of division/modulo by 0xc80 for grid math. No floating-point collision—pure tile snapping.

subsystems.name: Camera/View Management

representative_vas.role: Camera scroll/follow (748B) with inertial movement toward target
View boundary constraint (1082B) clamping camera to map limits
Camera interpolation (778B) smoothing movement with easing
Screen shake/effect (1076B) applying offset to view

subsystems.notes: Camera stored at in_ECX + 700 (offset for view struct). Uses incremental movement with 200-unit acceleration/deceleration. Separates X and Y. Map bounds stored at in_ECX + 0x2c1030/0x34 (width/height in tiles). All math integer-based, no rotation.

subsystems.name: Enemy/Actor Spawning

representative_vas.role: Enemy wave spawner (2458B) with encounter tables and randomization
Actor placement (1724B) positioning entities relative to tile grid
Actor lookup by ID (62B) querying global actor list

subsystems.notes: Actors stored in array at DAT_008a9b50 + 0x2790/0x27a0 with count at +0x2788. Actor struct has field at +0x1d8 for actor ID. Spawning uses random rolls on encounter table (ref: FUN_005bf505). Places actors into array, increments counter modulo 0xffffff. Calls FUN_00557370 to instantiate actor objects. Grid placement via tile divisions.

subsystems.name: Battle Animation System

representative_vas.role: Monster/battle animator (8KB) dispatching 50+ animation types (0x186a1-0x186b0)
Large animation helper (2187B) with timing and effect chains
Enemy hit/damage anim (936B) triggering knockback and state changes

subsystems.notes: Monolithic switch on animation type. Each case sets fields: in_ECX[0x8a] (animation index), in_ECX[0x7f] (duration frames?), in_ECX[0x3d/0x3e] (color/effect params). Calls FUN_00426620 for sprite setup, FUN_00426d70 for sound. No separate animation state machine—all in-function.

subsystems.name: Game State & UI

representative_vas.role: Level-up notification display (461B) formatting player text and updating UI
Game loop step/update (1449B) calling subsystem ticks
State transition handler (394B)

subsystems.notes: Global UI text buffer at local_200 [512]. Calls FUN_005bf3ee for string formatting with player name. Updates UI via FUN_0040b710 (graphics submit). Game state accessed at in_ECX + 0x9f4 (active flag). UI display list at DAT_008a9b50 + 0x1024 with 8-slot circular buffer.

subsystems.name: Utility/Math

representative_vas.role: 2D rotation calc (145B) using sin/cos with angle in millidegrees
Actor list search (62B) linear scan by actor ID
State validation (76B) checking combat readiness

subsystems.notes: Rotation uses _DAT_008a9a38 constant (likely 2*pi/1000 or similar degree-to-radian scale). Math is pure integer fixed-point, no SIMD. No separate math library—inlined throughout.

quirks.title: Hardcoded Scene IDs in Switch Constants
quirks.desc: Scene selection uses enum-like constants (0x186ca, 0x186cb, ..., 0x186d9, 0x186a1-0x186b0) that appear only in these dispatchers. No global symbol or resource table found. These IDs likely come from asset files or are baked into scripting bytecode—rebuilding the port will require discovering the ID-to-name mapping or rewriting sequencing.

quirks.title: Monolithic Scene Functions as State Machines
quirks.desc: Each scene is a single function call with 7-8 levels of nested if-else and switch on global state. No separate FSM; logic is imperative and linear. This makes porting a story scene expensive—must extract the intent and rebuild with higher-level scene scripting.

quirks.title: Tile Grid Stride = 0xc80 (3200 decimal) with Offset Formula (row*0x80+col)*0x10
quirks.desc: Collision grid is indexed via complex stride: (row*128 + col)*16. This is unusual—suggests either a custom memory layout or packing (16 bytes per tile struct). Likely encodes tile flags, height, water, etc. Reversing the stride is load-bearing for porting collision.

quirks.title: Circular Actor List with Counter Modulo 0xffffff
quirks.desc: Actor spawn counter at DAT_008a9b50 + 0x27a0 wraps at 0xffffff, not at array size. This allows unlimited spawns across frame but wastes memory if old actors aren't cleaned. Likely a performance trade-off (avoid expensive deletion, just overwrite on wrap).

quirks.title: No Audio/Input in This Band—All Deferred to Called Functions
quirks.desc: Game logic dispatches audio play (FUN_004024e0, FUN_00426d70) but does not inline DSound calls. Audio is purely callback-driven. Same for input—no keyboard/gamepad reads visible; logic uses pre-set button flags set elsewhere.

quirks.title: Fixed-Point Color Params in Animation (in_ECX[0x3d/0x3e])
quirks.desc: Animation type switch cases set in_ECX[0x3d] and [0x3e] to values like DAT_008a92b8, DAT_008a9308, etc. These are global color/effect constants. Animations reference them by pointer, not by value—suggests a palette or effect shader table. Port must recover the palette format.

maps.one_liner: Title scene runner, game options/menus, sprite/wave loaders, and core input/display initialization dominate this band.

subsystems.name: Title Scene Runner & Game Loop

representative_vas.role: Main title-scene state machine: fade in, wait for input, handle menu selection (difficulty picker, new game, continue)
Gameplay scene/map runner: handles player input, enemy spawns, magic-circle rendering, wave progression

subsystems.notes: State-machine pattern with tight frame-sync loop (GetTickCount). Stages: fade-in→wait-input→selected→game-start. Uses globals DAT_008a93cc (ZDD display core), DAT_008a93e4 (asset loader), DAT_008a6e80 (device/config ptr). No obvious vtable; this-calls are rare. Port difficulty: HIGH—state machine requires faithful millisecond timing and heavy coupling to graphics/input subsystems.

subsystems.name: Settings & Options Menus
