representative_vas.role: Top-level options dispatcher by param: screen/sound/game settings (0x1f-0x28), difficulty picker, vs-mode setup
Input-device key-binding UI: renders gamepad/keyboard key config menu, handles conflict detection

subsystems.notes: Heavy string-copy boilerplate. Uses FUN_0040f1c0 (dialog ctor), FUN_004124d0 (choice-list ctor). DAT_008a6e80 is config singleton (offsets +0x24=screen mode, +0x40=gfx quality, +0x21c=audio device, +0xa5e=input dev count). Enum param_1 in 0x564780 indexes major menu screens. Port difficulty: MEDIUM—menu logic is linear; main challenge is string resource (1024+ char temp buffers), not algorithm.

subsystems.name: Sprite & Wave Asset Loaders

representative_vas.role: Title-scene sprite-slot initializer: pre-allocates 23 ar_sprite_slot instances for character/effect assets (resource IDs 0x592-0x5df)
Primary engine initialization: sets up ZDD (display), ZDS (sound), ZDM (music), ZDI (sprite), loads palette, creates sprite instances with timing

subsystems.notes: 0x56e190 is purely setup boilerplate—initializes DAT_008a7ce4 et al. (sprite slot pointers). 0x562ea0 is massive (4062 bytes) init sequence: validates area info (<0x400 regions OK), pre-allocates ~900 sprite-slot+wave structures (loop at +0x226), loads 5 palette-palettes at offsets into DAT_008a9410, performs 20 sprite instances (DAT_008a92b8-DAT_008a936c) with timed fade in. Uses log_singleton (DAT_008a6620). Port difficulty: MEDIUM—mostly allocation. Critical: DAT_008a6e80 must be initialized before 0x562ea0 (config ptr, used for screen-res fallback logic).

subsystems.name: Input Device Initialization & Configuration

representative_vas.role: Input device state init: zero out 11 button-state dwords and 64 input-slot pointers (via in_ECX ptr)
Options main menu: builds option-list UI for Game/Graphics/Sound/Keyboard/Gamepad setup with conditional enable/disable logic

subsystems.notes: 0x56bfd0 is a 30-byte thiscall initializer (likely input_device ctor stub). 0x568de0 uses FUN_0040f1c0 (dialog), FUN_004134f0 (choice list), and FUN_0040f800 (label builder). Checks DAT_008a93dc/DAT_008a93d8 (gamepad device ptrs) to enable/disable Gamepad/Analog menus. Port difficulty: LOW—just null-init; challenge is mapping to modern input APIs.

subsystems.name: Configuration & Settings Storage

representative_vas.role: Config-to-string lookup: maps (subsystem_id, value_id) pairs to localized string pointers (difficulty names, button names, etc.)
Option value picker loop: manages state for selecting difficulty, screen resolution, sound volume with cursor/confirm/cancel input

subsystems.notes: 0x566a80 is a massive switch (case 3→5, case 4→15, case 0x2d→0x48, etc.) using string-table ptrs (DAT_00854d00, etc.) and format strings (s_Button_N, s_Difficulty_M). 0x565d10 loops until user selects/cancels, calls FUN_0055f550 (frame-sync), renders via FUN_0048c820. DAT_008a6e80 holds config state (referenced as *DAT_008a6e80 + offset for device-specific fields). Port difficulty: MEDIUM—complex enum mapping; requires full string table and rendering hooks.

subsystems.name: Menu & Dialogue UI Rendering

representative_vas.role: Small helper: unclear (47 bytes, likely render/cleanup stub)
Small helper: unclear (45 bytes, likely input handling or state check)

subsystems.notes: These are tiny; actual UI logic lives in FUN_0040f1c0, FUN_0040fa00 (label set), FUN_00412160 (option add). Rendering is delegated to FUN_005b9410 (likely frame begin), FUN_0048c820 (render to display), FUN_005b8fc0 (flip buffer). The FUN_0055f550 (frame-sync) wrapper is called in tight loops. Port difficulty: HIGH—depends on graphics subsystem port.

subsystems.name: Game Difficulty & Mode Selectors

representative_vas.role: Multi-setting picker: displays list of game settings by ID (difficulty, auto-guard, gfx quality, etc.), builds option menu dynamically
Unknown large function (964 bytes, likely mode or difficulty sub-screen)

subsystems.notes: 0x567ba0 uses FUN_00566570 (option name getter), FUN_00412160 (add-to-menu), FUN_00411940 (list ctor). Uses FUN_005657f0 to reset config values on 'default' button. Uses DAT_008a6e80 for all config-field queries. Port difficulty: MEDIUM—config struct is central; offset mapping is load-bearing.

quirks.title: Massive string-buffer allocation in every menu function
quirks.desc: Nearly every options/menu function allocates a 512-byte local_200[] buffer and calls string memcpy loops manually instead of strcpy (using pointer arithmetic + count). This is the decompiler's artifact of hand-unrolled MSVC string ops, but it's load-bearing: the actual buffer size is tight—no guard against overflow. A port must either use safe wrappers or copy the exact allocation strategy.

quirks.title: Implicit state-machine label jumps in title-scene loop
quirks.desc: LAB_0056bb55 at line 141 is an indirect-jump dispatcher via computed address (&PTR_DAT_0056bfa4)[local_64]. This implements a state-machine using a function-pointer table. The comment 'WARNING: Could not recover jumptable at 0x0056bb55. Too many branches' suggests Ghidra gave up recovering it. The actual vtable is at DAT_0056bfa4 with 12+ entries (local_64 ranges 0-10). A C port must recover this table by hand or risk silent jumps to wrong handlers.

quirks.title: Tight millisecond timing in title-scene loop with manual state transitions
quirks.desc: The title-scene loop calls GetTickCount() every frame, compares to manual fade timers (local_20, local_2c, local_34, local_68), and adjusts fade-out/fade-in (local_30, local_58) in 0x14/0x20-unit steps. The transition logic checks (local_30 < 0x10) and (local_68 < 0x32) to advance states. This is fragile: if frame timing shifts, the fade sequences will desync visually. A port cannot replace these with semantic 'fade for N milliseconds' without re-tuning all hardcoded transition counters.

quirks.title: Indirect offset computation for sprite/input slot access
quirks.desc: Both functions use patterns like *(int *)(iVar7 + 0x108) to access sprite slots in a dynamically allocated array via the object pointer (in_ECX[1]). The offsets (0xc, 0x10c, 0x110, 0x114, 0x118) are hardcoded field positions within a sprite-manager or input-device class. These offsets are NOT validated and must match the exact C++ layout (padding, vtable, member order). A port that reshuffles struct members will crash silently.

quirks.title: Enum-driven config subsystem IDs with magic numbers
quirks.desc: Configuration subsystems are identified by 16-bit param_1 values: 3=Difficulty, 4=AutoGuard, 5=Gfx, 6=DisplayFPS, 0x2e=Gamepad Sens, 0x2f=Analog Sens, 0x30-0x48=Keyboard Keys. The enum is not defined in the decompile output; these IDs are scattered across calls to FUN_00566a80 and FUN_00412160 throughout the menu code. A port must cross-reference with data sections to recover the full enum.

quirks.title: Per-frame implicit global mutation in game-loop via pointer indirection
quirks.desc: This function reads/writes game state via *DAT_008a6e80 (config ptr) and DAT_008a93cc (ZDD ptr) without explicit synchronization. If multiple threads or re-entrant calls occur, the state machine will corrupt. The original was single-threaded; a port must either preserve single-threaded guarantee or add locks around these globals.

quirks.title: Palette generation with floating-point cosine tables at init
quirks.desc: The function calls fcos() to generate 256 cosine samples, scales them, and writes to DAT_008a9410. This is done once at init to pre-compute fade/rotate math. The loop does double-precision FP arithmetic (float10) and truncates via __ftol(). A port must replicate this exactly or use precomputed tables; floating-point precision differences will cause visible palette jitter.

maps.one_liner: 2D sprite/animation and tileset rendering system clustered around master sprite init (57ca40), scene refresh (587e00), tile rendering (586010), and animation pumps (58f360).

subsystems.name: Sprite animation & batch management

representative_vas.role: Master sprite group 3 slot register (24884B, 233 slots + 94 clones + info-pool init)
Sprite group 0 register (2342B, tileset sprites)
SFX slot registration (6073B, 44 sound resource slots)
Tile layer draw + area init (6133B, palette rendering, 8x tile grid setup)

subsystems.notes: ar_sprite_slot class; cluster of 909-entry pool allocators at 0x8a760c (slots) + 0x8a8440 (info-entries). Per 0057ca40-rabbit-hole.md: pool[i] = slot[i] one-to-one shadow. Quirk: Ghidra byte-typed vs short-typed DAT rendering affects offset interpretation (byte offset always meaningful, not source-level offset). Key globals: DAT_008a760c (sprite slot table), DAT_008a8440 (info-entry pool), DAT_008a6b60 (SS_MGR / input_mgr singleton).

subsystems.name: Scene/map initialization & refresh

representative_vas.role: Scene refresh (18055B, multi-sprite data-ptr swap, palette codec setup)
Audio/music subsystem init (17310B, sotesw.dll load, temp WMA transcode)
Tile animation state machine (3341B, frame advance + palette effects)

subsystems.notes: FUN_00587e00 iterates info-entry ranges and swaps in new PE rdata const pointers for data field (+8 offset). FUN_0058d460 manages tile animation frame counters. Key global: DAT_008a9b50 (paint/scene context ~10KB). Port difficulty: scene-refresh consumer functions (FUN_00587e00, FUN_0058d460) are unported; info-entry pool is partially modelled but data-ptr semantics deferred until ported.

subsystems.name: Rendering pump & animation tick

representative_vas.role: Main animation frame pump (3030B, ~40 FPS tick, input dispatch, tile/sprite tick)
Sprite animation state machine (1823B, frame advance + palette effect code dispatch)
Sprite collision check (658B, 8-cell grid lookup for active sprite)

subsystems.notes: 58f360 is the top-level frame loop: GetTickCount() state machine (local_5c: 0=init, 1=tick, 2=sleep), tile grid tick (FUN_0048c150), input handling (FUN_0043c110), character update (FUN_00468a20). 58e780 tracks animation state (DAT_008a9400, DAT_008a9404) and triggers flag-conditional code paths. Quirk: heavy use of GetTickCount() delta-time (no frame-lock, self-paced). Port difficulty: medium (top-level tick structure is clear but animation state machine has cryptic multi-state handoff).

subsystems.name: GDI text & overlay

representative_vas.role: GDI slot font init (340B, LOGFONTA + CreateFontIndirectA)
GDI slot helper (189B)
GDI slot cleanup (195B)

subsystems.notes: ar_gdi_slot class; capacity 1..N font objects per slot. LOGFONTA construction with family dispatch (0=Courier, 3=Times, 4=Arial, + italic variants). Port difficulty: low (standard Win32 GDI, no engine-specific quirks).

subsystems.name: Sound system (SFX & ambient)

representative_vas.role: SFX slot batch (6073B, 44 sound resource IDs in DAT_008a6eXX globals)
Ambient sound slot batch (451B, 13 sound resources)
Sound slot helper (741B)
Sound volume/play control (1065B)

subsystems.notes: ar_sound_slot class; each function is a batch register of constant resource IDs + group/settings params. Slots live in BSS at 0x8a6efc..0x8a70c0 (40+ per function). Port difficulty: low (straightforward table-driven init, no state machine).

quirks.title: Ghidra typing ambiguity in info-entry pool writes
quirks.desc: DAT_008aXXXX variables typed inconsistently (byte* vs short*) by Ghidra. Source-level offsets like +2, +4, +8 do NOT match byte offsets when DAT is short-typed. Retail always uses +4 (flag field), +8 (data ptr), never +2. Byte offsets are invariant; source offsets are rendering artifacts.

quirks.title: GetTickCount()-based frame pump with soft cap
quirks.desc: No frame lock; tick runs whenever GetTickCount() delta exceeds threshold (0x3e9 ms = ~1000/1024 Hz). Tight loop with FPS counter (local_44) but no vsync. Drift accumulates over long sessions (no correction loop).

quirks.title: Animation state machine with cryptic multi-state handoff
quirks.desc: DAT_008a9400 (frame counter) and DAT_008a9404 (saved state) drive state changes. Three magic thresholds (0x28, 0x31, 0x32) with 300-second debounce (DAT_008a9408). Semantics unclear without animation asset table context.

quirks.title: Pool index aliasing: sprite slot pool == info-entry pool base address
quirks.desc: Both pools (0x8a760c for slots, 0x8a8440 for info-entries) are fields of input_mgr at +0x0aac (slots) and +0x18e0 (info). 909-entry unified allocation. off-by-one risk: pool[0] at 0x8a760c is a sentinel; real use starts at pool[1].

quirks.title: Stack-based dangling pointer checks
quirks.desc: Code compares local stack addresses (e.g., &stack0x00000000, &stack0xfffffffc) in control flow. Decompiler artifact or deliberate UB? Likely codegen result of complex control flow; semantically these comparisons always evaluate same (dangling reference).

maps.one_liner: Game scene/battle engine core: inventory/item management, party status menus, effect system with sparse sprite rendering and audio cue coordination

subsystems.name: Inventory & Item System

representative_vas.role: item list menu (dual-branch: regular vs special items)
item action handler (equip/remove/discard UI dispatch)
character-specific action menu builder
confirmatory action dialog (equip/discard display)

subsystems.notes: Structured as call chain from a central menu router (FUN_00591120). Methods access party data via `in_ECX + 0x4000/0x4010` offsets (inventory slots: 256-item array at 0x201c base). Item struct: offset 0xab=rarity(500 threshold), 0x1e=equippability, 0x16=curse status. Implements cond logic (cursed/rare items trigger confirmation loops). Dialog creation via FUN_00411940/FUN_0040f1c0; calls string builders FUN_005bf3ee for formatted prompts. Hard-coded constant 0x5f5e168 filters out dummy/invisible party members.

subsystems.name: Audio Cue System

representative_vas.role: reserve wave manager (processes audio queue)
audio idle/wait check with frequency limiter
audio track selector (8-slot search, param validation)
audio frequency throttle (countdown + capacity check)

subsystems.notes: Manages reserve audio buffer: offset 0x1b0 (buffer list ptr), 0x1b4 (count, 16-bit). Per-cue struct stride 0x24 bytes (offsets: +0x18=enabled, +0x1c=vol_adj). Implements capacity-aware allocation with modulo wrap at offset 0x1024 (8 slots: (idx+1)%8). Audio driver thresholds: 300ms min playback time (offset 0xa64*1000 / offsets 0xaac+0xa94+0xa68). Calls FUN_005bb8xx (audio-low) for setup/teardown. References DAT_008a6ed8 (queue head/tail tracker).

subsystems.name: Party Status/Menu System

representative_vas.role: title scene main menu loop (case dispatcher for 7 subscenes)
battle setup frame init (sprite coordinate setup, palette init, large local buffer 1536 bytes)
ability/skill menu
system options menu (quick heal, money display, give up)
party command menu (formation, follow mode, magic usage)

subsystems.notes: Rooted in scene dispatch pattern. Uses FUN_004124d0 (window frame factory) to build bordered menu boxes; FUN_00412c40 attaches labels. Offsets in party ecx: 0x3 = party member ptrs array, 0x4010/0x4030 = 256-item inventory heads. Character flags at offset 0x5c = 'alive' status. Status menu calls FUN_0055f190 for input polling; returns codes 0xb(cancel), 0xc(confirm), 0xd(retype), 0xe-0x13(action buttons). Menu internals use FUN_0040f800/FUN_0040fa00 for option labels. Theme strings from globals (e.g. DAT_008a2ebc = 'Messages', DAT_008a2eb4 = 'System').

subsystems.name: Sprite Rendering (Scene Frame Buffer)

representative_vas.role: primary render dispatch (13690B, complex compositing)
scene render pump (45963B, massive loop integrator)
sprite buffer allocation & setup (3522B, 8-sprite pool init)

subsystems.notes: Scene render-to-backbuffer: allocates 0x4120B sprite work structs (offset +0x4030 = 8 sprite ptrs). FUN_005a4770 is the mega-loop: polls input, ticks scene state, swaps buffers. Uses FUN_005a00c0 as frame compositor. Sprite stride 0xeec bytes (detected from FUN_0059f2c0 allocation loop). Memory layout includes 8x8 sprite grid cache (0x430 shorts per slot), 0x1800B sprite metadata arena, 0x2400B secondary buffer pool. Calls FUN_005a3de0 (cleanup), FUN_005a3e20 (sprite tick), FUN_005a3ff0 (render). Palette session attached at ecx[3]; backbuffer handle via DAT_008a6e80+0x254. No detected pixel-direct manipulation in this band (handled 0x5b range).

subsystems.name: Scene Transition & Load

representative_vas.role: area/scene loader (new game / continue handoff)
intro cinematic scene pump (924B state machine)
scene segment controller (1033B orchestrator)
scene cleanup/unload (577B teardown)

subsystems.notes: FUN_0059ec30 allocates per-scene context (0x18 bytes: [0]=resource ptr, [1..3]=asset ids, [4..5]=aux state). Calls FUN_0059f2c0 to allocate 0x4120B scene struct; parameter 2 is scene/area id (0x3f2=1010 for continue point). Unpacks asset ptrs from result and validates non-null before FUN_005a4320/FUN_005a4440 (unload routines). Intro scene FUN_0059e230 builds 3-stage sequence array on stack (local_124, local_98); nested cinematic loop. Uses FUN_005bef0e to free resources. DAT_008a9b50 is global scene state singleton; offset 0x1024 is slot index (modulo 8).

quirks.title: Modulo-based Audio Slot Wrapping
quirks.desc: Audio queue uses a hand-rolled modulo-wrap at `(idx + 1) & 0x80000007` with a sign-extension cleanup `((uVar4 - 1 | 0xfffffff8) + 1)` to handle wrapping within 8 slots. This is a non-obvious micro-optimization that could silently fail if the slot size assumption changes.

quirks.title: Hard-coded Dummy Member Filter
quirks.desc: Party member validity check compares `offset+0x9f0` against constant `0x5f5e168` to skip dummy/placeholder party slots. This magic number is unexplained and will break if party struct definition changes.

quirks.title: Nested String-copying in Menu Builders
quirks.desc: String length loops repeatedly re-scan full strings backward from terminator instead of using `strlen()` — multiple functions contain identical `while (*pcVar != '\0') ... ~uVar8` patterns, suggesting copy-paste code or macro expansion from a compiler.

quirks.title: Conditional Palette Mode Override
quirks.desc: Sprite color mode set based on check `*(int *)(*DAT_008a6e80 + 0x254)` — if true, forces palette indices 0x14/0x18 to 0xff (opaque white). Palette session is managed externally, creating a subtle dependency on initialization order.

quirks.title: Audio Frequency Limiter with Off-by-One Risk
quirks.desc: Countdown at `ecx+0x4074` decrements by 1 per tick, floors at 0. Rate limiting uses hard-coded 0x10 (16 ticks, ~256ms at 60Hz assumed). Checking `0 < *(int *)(ecx + 0x40e4)` with manual decrement then AND-mask `(0 - 1)` suggests a zero-guard that could fail if timing assumptions drift.

quirks.title: Scene Context Pointer Array Management
quirks.desc: Scene loader allocates 0x18B context struct, stores resource ptrs at indices [1..5] and validates [0], then unconditionally calls FUN_005bef0e on success. Use-after-free risk if FUN_005bef0e is called twice, or if resource cleanup order is incorrect.

quirks.title: Item Rarity Threshold at 500
quirks.desc: Rare items (offset 0xab < 500) trigger extended confirmation dialog. Threshold is hard-coded; no in-game parameter lookup. Quest items with identical rarity value may behave differently if additional flags exist.

maps.one_liner: Game state mechanics (abilities, attributes, character management) + launcher config parsing + ZDD renderer + bitmap session + utility string/memory infrastructure dominate this band.

subsystems.name: Launcher Configuration Parser

representative_vas.role: gigantic config.dat XOR-deobfuscation + multi-DLL (sotesd/w/p) signature validator and asset registry. Failed decompile (timeout) at 45963B.
13690B initialization pump for launcher settings, registry/file I/O, DirectDraw mode enumeration
cleanup/dtor for launcher state resources (PTR_DAT_008a9b50 main engine struct cleanup)
event logging subsystem for debug trace (writes to in-ECX[0x1800..] event ring buffer)

FUN_005a4770 is the launch entrypoint—parses sotesd.dll signature, enumerates resolutions, populates PTR_DAT_008a9b50+0x1160 (32x32-slot entity registry). in_ECX offsets: +0x170, +0x164, +0x178, +0x17c = asset/config memory pools. 405B entity spatial collision (NPC/door bounds check vs player pos, alignment with in_ECX[0x1dc] faction logic)

subsystems.notes: Vtable: Heavy use of in_ECX (this-call) as character object pointer. Key globals: DAT_008a9b50 (entity registry), DAT_008a9b64 (dialogue state), offsets tracked: in_ECX+0x750 (stats struct), in_ECX+0xc794 (??). String refs: '%s %s recovered %d HP', '%s strength returns', etc. Port difficulty: MODERATE-HIGH (ability enum switch cases have recursive calls, string formatters, complex stat calcs with fixed-point 0xf bit-shifts for time scaling).

representative_vas.role: 1124B __thiscall sprite-registration: params include animation type (7 cases), facing (0-2), spawn pos, duration. Writes to iVar16[0x15ab8..0x15ae8] (sprite animation state struct). Calls FUN_005b1c50 entity-collision, FUN_005b5600 dtor-link.

30B LCG RNG (DAT_008a4f94 *= 0x343fd; += 0x269ec3; >> 0x10 & 0x7fff)
11B heap-free wrapper (thunk to FUN_005c123f, outside range)
241B file I/O (GetFileVersionInfoA, queries PE version resource)

subsystems.name: Version & Launcher Dialog Support

representative_vas.role: 26B cleanup: if (*in_ECX != 0) FUN_005bef0e(*in_ECX); *in_ECX = 0
1075B version info file read + string manipulation (GetFileVersionInfoSizeA, copies description/file-ver/company into param_1+0x80..0xd0). Error handling branches via string concatenation.
186B launcher-mode-config apply (reads param_1, writes to in_ECX struct at various offsets)

subsystems.notes: Loose cluster supporting FUN_005a4770 launcher config. Port difficulty: LOW.

quirks.title: FUN_005a4770 remains undecompiled due to massive size (45963B timeout)
quirks.desc: This is the true launcher entry—XOR-deobfuscation of config.dat, multi-DLL asset signature verification, resolution enumeration. Port must tackle this via binary analysis or symbol recovery. Already flagged in launcher-dialog.md.

quirks.title: RNG is a simple LCG seeded by global DAT_008a4f94
quirks.desc: FUN_005bf505() does *0x343fd + 0x269ec3 on DAT_008a4f94. Used pervasively for sprite effect timing, ability cooldown timing, damage variance. Port must preserve seed initialization order.

quirks.title: Ability/spell system is hard-dispatch on effect enum (9/10/11/12/13/18/19 cases)
quirks.desc: Each case calls into a sub-system (damage calc, MP drain, item apply, status apply). Cases 9/10 appear most common (heal/buff). No polymorphism—straight switch-case tower.

quirks.title: Entity collision uses modulo-masked iteration (uVar7 & 0x80000007 with sign-correction)
quirks.desc: Iterates through DAT_008a9b50+0x1160 (32-entity table) with modulo 0x20 mask. Suggests max 32 simultaneous entities in scope (likely on-screen).

quirks.title: Sprite animation stores per-char offsets in a compact 0x200-byte region (chars encoded as offset indices, not strings)
quirks.desc: Converts ASCII char - 0x21 -> 2-byte offset. Space=0xff sentinel. Max 40 chars per sprite (0x200 / 4 = 80 offsets, half used). Load-bearing struct offset assumption.

quirks.title: DAT_008a9b50 is the central engine-state global (appears 32+ times in range)
quirks.desc: Contains: +0x1160 = 32-slot entity registry; +0x1024..0x1044 = sub-arrays for character UI; +0x2784 = game-mode struct; +0x2798 = boss-ai state. Vtable-like indexing pattern. May be single global class instance.

quirks.title: Fixed-point math used for scaling timing (>> 0xf, *= 1000, /= 1000)
quirks.desc: Time delta scaled as (iVar5 * local_264 + (iVar5 * local_264 >> 0x1f & 0x7fffU)) >> 0xf. Off-by-one risk in rounding. Common in ability cooldown calc.

quirks.title: Bitmap conversion dispatch on biBitCount (8 vs 0x18 vs fallthrough to 0x20)
quirks.desc: Engine-quirks.md #12: '24-bit pixelformat case falls through to 32-bit'. FUN_005b7310 dispatches on in_ECX->biBitCount; 0x18 case has no break, falls to default (0x20 handling). Load-bearing quirk.

quirks.title: Event flag ring buffer at in_ECX[0x1800] with max 0x200 entries
quirks.desc: Debug trace buffer: (in_ECX + uVar2 * 0xc) = {event_id, param1, param2}. Wraps at 0x200. Only populated if +0x1800 != 0. Diagnostic-only.

quirks.title: Config.dat is XOR-obfuscated with per-byte key derived from plaintext header (engine-quirks.md #8)
quirks.desc: Launcher-dialog.md mentions config.dat parse is FUN_005a4770. Asset-loader.md #13 notes sotesd.dll carries 60-byte signature check. Both require reversing encryption key.

scouts.summary: The 3441-byte title-menu scene runner orchestrates the intro animation (studio/title fade-ins, press-button prompt) and hands off to the top-level menu (New Game/Continue/Options/Exit). It drives 7-phase state machine with frame-budget sync, maintains menu-controller and input state, and returns state codes to the outer dispatcher. This is the first visible user-facing frame and unblocks image-on-screen validation.

functions.role: Main outer loop driver — phase progression, state-var management (fade ramps, tick counters), frame-limiter integration, input polling, menu dispatch
Sparkle particle spawner — called during phase 7 to generate title-screen twinkle effects while fade continues
Frame-end flip bridge — renders sparkles and calls surface Flip; hotpath for committed title menu frames
Sprite animator — updates sprite positions/rotation/fade for intro logos and menu items based on state flags
Scene teardown — cleanup on exit (unload surfaces, waves, GDI slots, release menu-controller)
Menu-controller allocator — lazy-init; returns handle to menu UI state for input routing
Input button poll — checks if a button was pressed within time window (100ms debounce)
Input action latch — converts button press to menu action enum (navigate/confirm/cancel)
Ambient audio pump — drives background music fade/volume transitions during scenes
Input device tick — syncs joystick timestamps and polls state before menu polling
Scene reset/cleanup (sprite array + menu state wipe) — called on intro skip or before menu handoff
Menu-controller state initializer — zeros flags and UI slot pointers
Graphics mode reset (ZDD state setup) — ported; prerequisite for FUN_0056c180
Surface touch (DDraw Restore if needed) — ported; called before fade drawing on phase transitions
Surface Flip call — ported; final frame commit via IDirectDrawSurface7::Flip
Input device init hook — ported; called when menu confirm attaches joystick pads

scouts.blocks: First visible frame (title splash and menu appear on screen); unblocks DDraw + scene-dispatcher validation; paves way for gameplay scene runners

quirks.title: Frame-limiter integration via local_28 sub-state machine
quirks.desc: Nested 3-state pump (0=init, 1=ready, 2=waiting) that calls FUN_005b1030 and tracks tick budget in milliseconds. The outer loop must not skip pump calls; frame-budget underrun causes intro animation to stall.

quirks.title: Joystick lazy-init only on menu confirm
quirks.desc: DInput pads are NOT enumerated at boot. Engine calls FUN_005ba120 only on action iVar14==3 (confirm key). A headless run that never reaches 'press start' will never attach input devices — this is intentional, not a bug.

quirks.title: Fade-ramp multiplier arithmetic
quirks.desc: Each phase uses unsigned 32-bit fade (uVar15, range 0..1000) incremented per frame. Clamp before/after each phase's arithmetic to prevent overflow. Phase 5+6 use conditionals to cap increments (lines 305-309, 322-326).

quirks.title: Phase handler dispatch is NOT a switch — it's an indirect jump table
quirks.desc: Call via PTR_DAT_0056bfa4[local_64]() is a function-pointer array, not a fallthrough switch. Ghidra could not recover the table; radare2 recovers 7 distinct handler VAs covering 11 phase indices (0-10). Inlining handlers is possible but requires strict phase bounds checking.

quirks.title: Input button debounce window is 100ms
quirks.desc: FUN_0043c110 rejects button presses if timestamp (param_1) is not within 100ms of latched press time. Calling site (GetTickCount) must be accurate; synchronization with input-device tick (FUN_00566250) is implicit, not enforced.

quirks.title: Menu slots populated in fixed order: 0x1a, 0x1c, 0x1e, 0x1d, 8
quirks.desc: The 5 menu items (New Game, Load, Options, ?, Exit) are hardcoded in sequence. The loop at line 451-464 matches slot action_id against DAT_008a6e80+0xa60 to set the 'selected' index. Offset 0xa60 is a save-game validity flag — changing order or omitting a slot breaks menu init.

scouts.summary: The menu-controller cluster manages all interactive list/menu UI across the engine: inventory dialogs, item selection, party status screens, and shop interfaces. The allocator FUN_00412c10 dispenses pre-pooled menu items; a companion container allocator FUN_004134f0 builds the list viewport and geometry. Input wiring via FUN_00412d30 and rendering callbacks (FUN_0040fa00, FUN_0040f5c0) compose the interactive loop, ultimately feeding action codes to scene drivers.

functions.role: Menu-item allocator from pool — returns pointer, sets up back-reference and index
Large menu-container allocator; sets up config structure, item array, render buffers
Add single item to menu; colors, rendering setup, callback wiring
Input-key mapping initializer; sets code 0-0xb for button/keyboard layout
Item state validator; checks party/character type flags at offset +0x9c4
Container (window) creation; manages current-selection index, last-item flag
Container geometry setup; wires viewport position/size into rendering parameters
NPC/shop item list population; walks 8-entry character array, filters visible items
Equipment/stats copy-down; replicas item data into display structure for rendering
Modal dialog builder; allocates container + menu, wires input loop via FUN_0055f190
Deposit-item scene (full gameplay function); uses menu controller for inventory selection
Withdraw-item scene (full gameplay function); counterpart to deposit, retrieves from storage
Item filtering & population logic; applies availability rules, renders per-selected slot
Modal choice dialog; input loop + text layout, returns selected index to caller

scouts.blocks: Inventory management, item selection dialogs, party status displays, shop/NPC interfaces — unlocks full game-facing menu functionality needed for playable scenes (title, inventory management, equipment swapping).

quirks.title: Pool-based allocation, not heap
quirks.desc: Menu items are allocated from a pre-sized object pool (ECX+0x48 array, max ECX+0x4c), not malloc. Pool must be pre-initialized by parent; porting requires understanding parent object lifetime and pool sizing constraints.

quirks.title: Double-pointer config structure
quirks.desc: Config, items, and render buffers stored as pointers at fixed offsets [0x5d], [0x5e], [0x5f] in the menu object, but config itself is heap-allocated; accessing config[...] requires deref of the pointer first, not direct array indexing.

quirks.title: Index-before-increment anomaly
quirks.desc: Container increments selection index at offset+6 AFTER storing it, but the menu item captures the OLD index. Edge case: first item gets index 0, last gets (count-1), but reads can be off-by-one if not careful about when increment happens.

quirks.title: Unsigned ushort masking
quirks.desc: Multiple places truncate with `(index & 0xffff)` to convert back to ushort, implying loop-counter overflow risks or sign-extension bugs in original code. Port must preserve exact bit-width semantics.

quirks.title: RGB color encoding without alpha
quirks.desc: Colors hardcoded as 0xffffff (white), 0x455f7b (orange). No alpha channel or transparency bits visible; rendering backend likely does modulation or multiply-blend, not per-pixel alpha.

scouts.summary: Input polling and action latching for keyboard and gamepad. Core polling function scans a time-stamped circular event buffer; action latch detects UI input (confirm/cancel/nav) and translates to gamepad-independent action codes. Requires DInput device state buffer initialization (GetDeviceState vtable offset 0x24) and the per-device wrapper from DInput init (FUN_005ba120/FUN_005ba290).

functions.role: Button event poll—scans time-stamped circular buffer (offset +0x108, 64-entry ring) for pressed buttons matching a given ID and timestamp window (100ms tolerance). Returns 1 if found, clears the entry. Thiscall on input-controller this-object.

Input action latch—translates raw button states into gameplay actions (0-10: up/down/left/right/confirm/cancel/diagnostic). Checks DInput pad state (offset +0x5c points to gamepad/device state struct) and returns a resolved action code. Thiscall.

Menu navigation state machine—driven by action codes from FUN_0043ce50. Handles phase-based button repeat (100ms initial delay, then 300-100ms hold repeats) for menu cursor movement. Returns state changes (1=confirm, 2=confirm-with-refresh, etc). Thiscall on menu-controller context.

DInput gamepad lazy-attach—called on first menu-confirm to enumerate up-to-2 joystick devices. Allocates per-device wrapper struct, stores in slots &DAT_008a93dc[0..1]. Returns non-zero on success. See audio-init.md for wrapper layout; depends on FUN_005ba070/FUN_005ba540 (enumeration helpers).

IDirectInputDevice::Acquire wrapper—__thiscall on input_dev struct. Calls device vtable[0x1c] to acquire exclusive foreground binding. Sets acquired=1 flag (+0x08) on success. Already ported in wnd_proc_win32.c (wp_input_acquire placeholder).

unported_deps: 0x43c650 (gamepad axis query for UI—returns axis state enum; called by FUN_0043bca0)
0x43c920 (menu action latch for gamepad variant—alternate input source handling)
0x5ba540 (DInput device state polling—reads keyboard+gamepad and populates ring buffer at +0x108)
GetDeviceState vtable plumbing—the DInput keyboard device's vtable[0x24] (method 9) must be called somewhere to fill the keyboard state array; not yet located in decompile
Input buffer initialization—which VA fills the +0x108 ring buffer? Must be called in the frame pump after DInput acquire
scouts.blocks: Any interactive screen (title menu, save/load, options, gameplay). Title-scene.md notes that phase 8 (menu UI) calls FUN_0043c110 + FUN_0043ce50 each frame to handle user input; without this, the engine advances through the intro but never responds to button presses.

quirks.title: Ring buffer state machine
quirks.desc: The circular buffer at +0x108 uses a backwards linear search (63 down to 0) coupled with a head-index at +0x0c. Each slot is a 3-dword [button_id, timestamp, state]. When FUN_0043c110 finds a match with state==1, it clears state to 0 AND writes 0 to that offset in a separate array at +0x0c+idx*4. This is a consume-on-read pattern—each poll consumes the event once.

quirks.title: Menu action latching
quirks.desc: Thiscall reads offset +0x5c (gamepad state pointer) to check if param_1 action codes match current device state. Gamepad branch (param_1[2]==2) interprets device fields [3/5/6 = button flags, 1 = button count]. Returns 6 or 8 based on field +[6] decrement—countdown to auto-repeat. Keyboard branch (param_1[2]==1) delegates to FUN_0043ca40.

quirks.title: Lazy gamepad enumeration
quirks.desc: Not called at boot; only when menu-confirm (action==3) is first triggered. Allocates & acquires up to 2 DInput joystick devices. Stores in DAT_008a93dc[0] and [1]. If this is never called (e.g., headless turbo smoke test that exits before pressing start), gamepad input simply stays unavailable—no crash, correct per title-scene.md note.

quirks.title: DInput polling black box
quirks.desc: The decompile does not show FUN_005ba540 or the VA that calls IDirectInputDevice::GetDeviceState (vtable[0x24]). The ring buffer at +0x108 is written by SOMETHING, but Ghidra's decomp cuts off or the call is wrapped in a macro. Frida hook on GetDeviceState will reveal the caller. This is load-bearing for input to work at all.

quirks.title: Button repeat / debounce via timestamps
quirks.desc: Manages hold-repeat by checking timestamp delta from param_2 (current time). First press sets a 300ms freeze (+0x1c = time + 300), then on next call if time > frozen timestamp, switches to 100ms repeat window. This is ALL in C—no DirectInput repeat settings are used. Manual timer-based debounce.

scouts.summary: The wave-load cluster (FUN_00563ef0 plus its direct dependencies FUN_005bb250/FUN_005bb2f0) implements lazy sound-bank loading into DirectSound buffer pools. The first half (sound-slot field init) is ported as ar_sound_slot_init; the second half (wave decode + buffer fill) remains unported and requires DirectSound vtable method dispatch across 3-4 complex initialization steps. Together they feed the sprite and sound slot pools (DAT_008a760c and DAT_008a92b4) that track in-memory sprite/asset metadata. The format is uncompressed palette+pixel or wave data read from PE resources via chunked-memory abstraction (FUN_005b6340/FUN_005b68f0 handle streaming).

functions.role: ar_sound_slot_init + lazy wave-load dispatcher. First 110 bytes: field init (ported). Second 335 bytes: wave decode + DirectSound buffer creation (unported).
sound boot register batch — initializes 12 core UI/menu sound slots via ar_sound_slot_init. Ported.
game-sound register batch (group 3) — initializes 174 gameplay sound slots via ar_sound_slot_init. Ported.
Per-sprite-slot register — stamps resource_id/width/height/colorkey and allocates 1-entry ar_sprite_entry array. Core pool feeder. Ported.
Hundreds-sprite batch register — 260+ inlined ar_sprite_slot_register calls populating sprite IDs 62..863. Ported.
Boot driver — initializes all asset pools + calls sprite/sound batches. Ported.
Low-level PE resource read (FindResourceA/LoadResourceA/LockResourceA). Called by wave-load path.
Polymorphic asset reader (kind 1=PE res, 2=chunked stream, 3=file). Feeds sprite decode + wave load.
Chunked-memory-stream decoder (not a decompressor; abstracts the 676996-byte chunk-stride used by sotesd.dll multi-part resources).

unported_deps: 0x5bb250 (150 B) — wave decode stage 1: allocate DirectSound descriptor buffer + call FUN_005bb3d0 dispatcher
0x5bb2f0 (140 B) — wave decode stage 2: copy init-state buffer to next slot + call FUN_005bb5c0 (DuplicateSoundBuffer)
0x5bb3d0 (494 B) — DirectSound CreateSoundBuffer vtable wrapper; complex multi-DWORD state machine.
0x5bb380 (24 B) — simple DirectSound object init helper
0x5bb610 (unknown) — called by FUN_005bb3d0; presumed PE-resource reader setup
0x5bb740 (unknown) — called by FUN_005bb3d0; presumed chunked-stream reader setup
0x5b6340 (unknown) — polymorphic asset reader (PE resource / chunked stream / file)
scouts.blocks: Title-menu rendering (once scene runner FUN_0056aea0 is ported, it calls ar_sound_slot_init for lazy boot-bank loads during scene transitions; the second half is needed for actual sound-board playback in-game).

quirks.title: Uncompressed asset storage
quirks.desc: All assets in sotesd/sotesw/sotesp.dll are stored raw (uncompressed), just chunked at 676996-byte PE-resource boundaries. Extraction is straight byte-dump; no decompression step needed.

quirks.title: DLL signature checks
quirks.desc: All three DLLs (sotesd.dll, sotesw.dll, sotesp.dll) carry integrity checks: 60-byte / 8-byte / 8-byte strings encoded as (byte + 0x41). Engine verifies at boot (0x5af6ac, 0x575350 areas). Drop-in can skip or no-op these checks.

quirks.title: Chunked logical-blob (sotesd 1000–1004)
quirks.desc: Five PE resources (1000–1004) totalling ~2.2 MB are a single logical asset chunked at 676996-byte boundaries. FUN_005b67c0 transparently spans them via indexing into a chunk-pointer array. Extraction tools must join them back together.

quirks.title: Wave-load buffer allocation is lazy
quirks.desc: Sound slots are initialized at boot with buffer=NULL (FUN_005bb250 not called). The wave bytes are only loaded on-demand when the sound is first played (TBD where this happens in gameplay loop).

quirks.title: DirectSound error path branches
quirks.desc: If wave decode fails (FUN_005bb250 returns 0), the engine logs the failure, calls FUN_005bf4e8 to get a file handle for writing, then uses FUN_005bf496 to stamp a timestamped 'Failed_Wave_Load_*' message. This is error-recovery telemetry, not core audio.

scouts.summary: FUN_005a4770 is the 46 KB catch-all init subsystem that orchestrates launcher dialog presentation, user settings persistence (config.dat), window class registration, and main window creation. It reads XOR-obfuscated (key 0x88) config.dat from disk, registers 101 schema fields into a 2828-byte in-memory settings struct, populates the launcher dialog from saved state, and persists the user's choices back to config.dat upon exit. The cluster includes path builders (FUN_005b0ce0/5b0c60), the launcher dialog dispatch (FUN_005b0d70), and config loader helpers. Porting requires implementing the file I/O, XOR deobfuscation, schema field unpacking, and round-trip persistence.

functions.role: Main init orchestrator — parses cmdline, loads config.dat, runs launcher dialog, registers window class, creates main window. Too large for Ghidra decompile; requires radare2 disassembly slicing.
Schema array allocator — allocates 101*0x1c = 2828 bytes for per-field records; stores name + metadata.
Per-field schema register — writes field ID, default values, and dynamic allocation hints into schema array at index (field_id & 0xffff).
Config.dat value unpacker — iterates field-value pairs from decoded plaintext, locates matching schema entry, unpacks and stores values into in-memory struct.
Directory enumerator — reads a game-data subdirectory as key=value text pairs, used for config enumeration or asset listing.
Key lookup in schema table — binary search or sequential match for field name in 0x128-byte record array.
Path builder (settings) — concatenates game-dir + 'user' + 'config.dat' or similar config-related paths.
Path builder (general) — generic path concatenation for subdirs.
Launcher dialog wrapper — calls DialogBoxParamA(hInst, 0x2711, NULL, dlgproc=0x4013c0, 0); returns launcher dialog result.
Window title builder — assembles 'Fortune Summoners Ver1.2 - Product Ver.' from resource strings.
Main window creator — calls CreateWindowExA(0, CLASS_LIZSOFT_SOTES, …, WS_POPUP, 0,0,100,100, …).

unported_deps: 0x4013c0 (DLGPROC @ address, not function pointer; Ghidra missed it — radare2 disassembly only)
0x5b61b0 (initialization subroutine, called by config loader on first run)
0x5b65b0, 0x5b6db0, 0x5b6bc0 (resource/DDraw pre-init — outside scope of config parser)
Direct file I/O (no FUN_ wrapper found; likely wrapped inside 0x5b62xx slice)
scouts.blocks: --launcher-mode=N CLI flag, which currently stubs to hardcoded Windowed (mode 2). Full port enables reading the 4 launcher radio settings + all 101 keybinding/volume/difficulty/event fields from user/config.dat, persisting user choices, and wiring the dialog's state-machine scrape back to in-memory settings struct. Unblocks genuine multi-run settings persistence and deferred platform/difficulty selection (required for full Phase-2 playability).

quirks.title: Radio enums use 3/4/5, not 0/1/2
quirks.desc: Launcher dialogs for screen mode, VRAM, quality, and sound all use enum values 3 / 4 / 5 instead of 0 / 1 / 2. This offset appears both in the on-disk config.dat layout and the in-memory globals (DAT_008a9b48/4a/4c/4e). The same +3 offset must be faithfully preserved during round-trip load/save, else the dialog will fail to populate or persist.

quirks.title: config.dat is XOR-obfuscated with key 0x88
quirks.desc: The 840-byte file has a plaintext 16-byte header [hdr_size=16][version=0x2711][data_size=820][checksum] followed by 820 bytes of XOR-obfuscated body. The XOR key is the single byte 0x88. Deobfuscation is `b_plaintext = b_encrypted ^ 0x88`. The body is denser than the in-memory representation — unpacker must expand (u32, u32) pairs into the 0x1c-byte per-field schema records.

quirks.title: Schema registration happens at boot via 101 calls to FUN_005afb90
quirks.desc: On startup, FUN_005a4770 calls FUN_005afb00 to allocate a 2828-byte array, then calls FUN_005afb90 repeatedly (once per field) to register field ID, name, type hints, and default values. The in-memory layout is then used by the unpacker (FUN_005afc90) to deserialize the on-disk format. Reverse-engineering the exact sequence of FUN_005afb90 calls (field IDs, names, types) will require radare2 disassembly of FUN_005a4770 or Frida hook on the register path.

quirks.title: Zoom 1920x1440 radio is hidden at WM_INITDIALOG
quirks.desc: Control ID 0x272A (10026) exists in the dialog resource but is unconditionally hidden at WM_INITDIALOG with no path to re-enable it. The radio scrape path doesn't even check it. Zoom mode effectively supports only 1280x960 despite resource suggesting 1920x1440 option. Likely abandoned on 2012-era hardware or unfinished; port should omit the 1920x1440 branch.

quirks.title: First dword in plaintext (0xBC D9 34 B5) is unexplained
quirks.desc: The plaintext body begins with a 4-byte word that doesn't fit the (u32, u32) pair schema. Observed value 0xB534D9BC appears to be a hash, per-file salt, or checksum — its role is TBD. Must be preserved during round-trip load/save to avoid file corruption.

scouts.summary: Audio subsystem bring-up: DirectSound wrapper (ZDS) initialization for sound effects, and music manager (ZDM) setup for WMA BGM playback through Windows Media Foundation graph builder. The title BGM represents the smallest measurable audio milestone — just loading and playing one WMA track from a resource DLL through DirectShow/WMF without user input. Depends on asset loading and DirectSound initialization (already ported parts of boot sequence), but the playback graph construction (FUN_005bab10) and WMA-to-graph binding (FUN_005bc150) are new unported territories.

functions.role: ZDS (DirectSound wrapper) alloc + primary buffer creation; called from boot driver
ZDM (music manager) ctor; zeroes fields and copies engine name
ZDM voice-slot allocator; allocates 50×576 B voice pool + 8-channel metadata
Music track playback trigger; title BGM entry point — allocates voice slot, loads WMA via FUN_005bc150, feeds to graph
WMA resource extractor; pulls sotesw.dll resource ID to temp file, error reporting
Graph descriptor init; allocates WMF graph struct (0x338 B) and sets up playback metadata
WMF graph builder creation; CoCreateInstance + RenderFile for WMA, error strings (E_OUTOFMEMORY, VFW_E_*), ReleaseAndLog chain
Voice slot playback accessor; routes to graph builder (FUN_005bab10) or fallback (FUN_005baec0)
AddMusicTrack setup; binds track metadata (volume, fade, loop) to voice slot
Query: voice slots populated (is music playing)
Query: track exists at voice index
Query: track is loaded and state==2 (playable)
Volume setter; iterates voice slots, calls FUN_005bbdb0 to set fade/volume
SFX buffer allocator; calls FUN_005bb3d0 for per-effect wave buffer creation
Wave buffer descriptor creation; parse RIFF header, CreateSoundBuffer for DSound secondary buffer
Sound bank register; maps resource ID to SFX slot, lazy-loads wave buffers on demand

unported_deps: 0x5bb180 (DSound primary buffer setup, ported via FUN_00562ea0 boot)
scouts.blocks: Title scene BGM playback (first audio milestone). Unblocks: in-game music transitions, SFX mixing, audio pausing on window deactivate, volume slider in options menu.

quirks.title: WMA → temporary file pipeline
quirks.desc: Music tracks (sotesw.dll WMA resources) are NOT played directly from resource memory. Instead, FUN_005bc150 extracts resource bytes to a GetTempFileA path, then FUN_005bab10 calls WMF RenderFile on the temp file. Temp file is deleted after playback. This is a quirk: most games stream from memory or use an in-memory IStream wrapper; here the engine writes to disk even for a single track playback.

quirks.title: DirectShow/WMF hidden behind CoCreateInstance
quirks.desc: The audio graph is built via COM (CoCreateInstance with IID_IGraphBuilder + RenderFile), not the raw DirectShow C API. This means the entire graph-builder initialization is encapsulated in undocumented IIDs (DAT_00850f58, DAT_00850f08, DAT_00850f28). GUIDs must be recovered from the data section to port this correctly.

quirks.title: 50-voice polyphony ceiling
quirks.desc: ZDM allocates exactly 50 voice slots (param_1 = 0x32 hardcoded in audio-init.md line 49), each 576 bytes (0x240), plus per-voice metadata. This is generous for 2008 indie 2D, suggesting the engine tracks music + multiple SFX + ambience layers simultaneously.

quirks.title: Settings gate: 'Disable Sound' → skip ZDM only, not ZDS
quirks.desc: When launcher checkbox 'Disable Sound' is set, in-memory settings[0x21c] is 1, which gates ZDM init but NOT ZDS (DirectSound). So primary buffer + SFX still initialize; only music is disabled. This means harness can test SFX without music by toggling the checkbox.

quirks.title: No streaming or looping detection in decompile yet
quirks.desc: The FUN_005bcb80 → FUN_005bab10 chain calls RenderFile once and does not appear to handle loop-count or fade-out (FUN_005bbdb0 is called from FUN_005bbcd0 in title scene, suggesting volume/fade are external to the graph). Exact loop behaviour and crossfade timing TBD once FUN_005bbb50 (graph pump) is reverse-engineered.
